class="prose mt-32"><div class="!text-title-h5 [&amp;:not(:first-child)]:mt-72 mb-24 relative"><span class="absolute -top-[120px]" id="introduction"></span><h2 class="!text-title-h5">Introduction</h2></div>
<p>Web scraping is an essential skill for programmers in this data-driven world. Whether you&#x27;re tracking prices, monitoring competitors, or gathering research data, automated web scraping can save you countless hours of manual work. In this comprehensive guide, you&#x27;ll learn how to schedule and automate your Python web scrapers using completely free tools and services. If you&#x27;re building a scraper that targets paginated lists or product grids, check out our guide on <a class="!underline" href="/blog/list-crawling-python-beautifulsoup-scrapy-firecrawl">list crawling with Python</a> before automating it—getting the extraction right first makes scheduling far simpler.</p>
<div class="blog-heading relative [&amp;:not(:first-child)]:mt-48 mb-16"><span class="absolute -top-[120px]" id="why-automate-your-web-scraper?"></span><h3>Why automate your web scraper?</h3></div>
<p>Manual or one-off web scraping can be time-consuming and error-prone. You need to repeatedly run scripts, update data frequently, and sometimes work during off-hours just to gather information.</p>
<p>Scheduling your web scrapers automates this entire process. It collects data at optimal times without manual intervention, ensures consistency, and frees up your valuable time for actual data analysis rather than repetitive data gathering.</p>
<div class="blog-heading relative [&amp;:not(:first-child)]:mt-48 mb-16"><span class="absolute -top-[120px]" id="what-tools-can-you-use-to-automate-web-scrapers-in-python?"></span><h3>What tools can you use to automate web scrapers in Python?</h3></div>
<p>If you are building scrapers in Python, you have many completely free options to schedule them.</p>
<p>In terms of local scraping, Python&#x27;s <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">schedule</code> library is very intuitive and fastest to set up. There is also the built-in <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">asyncio</code> for concurrent scraping. There are also system-level automation tools like cron jobs for macOS/Linux and the Task Scheduler for Windows.</p>
<p>There are also many cloud-based solutions like GitHub Actions (completely free), PythonAnywhere (free tier), Google Cloud Functions (free tier), and Heroku (free tier with limitations).</p>
<p>In this guide, we will start with basic local scheduling and progress to using GitHub Actions, all while following best practices and ethical scraping guidelines.</p>
<p>Let&#x27;s begin by setting up your development environment and writing your first scheduled web scraper.</p>
<div class="blog-heading relative [&amp;:not(:first-child)]:mt-48 mb-16"><span class="absolute -top-[120px]" id="common-challenges-in-automating-web-scrapers"></span><h3>Common challenges in automating web scrapers</h3></div>
<p>Scheduling web scrapers is an easy process, as you will discover through this tutorial. The real challenge lies in ensuring that the scrapers don&#x27;t break the day after they are put on schedule. Common issues include websites changing their HTML structure, implementing new anti-bot measures, or modifying their robots.txt policies. Additionally, network issues, rate limiting, and IP blocking can cause scheduled scrapers to fail.</p>
<p>For these reasons, it is almost impossible to build long-lasting scrapers written in Python frameworks. But the web scraping landscape is changing as more AI-based tools are emerging, like <a class="!underline" href="https://www.firecrawl.dev">Firecrawl</a>.</p>
<p>Firecrawl provides an AI-powered web scraping API that can identify and extract data from HTML elements based on semantic descriptions in Python classes. While traditional scrapers rely on specific HTML selectors that can break when websites change, Firecrawl&#x27;s AI approach helps maintain scraper reliability over time.</p>
<p>For demonstration purposes, we&#x27;ll implement examples using Firecrawl, though the scheduling techniques covered in this tutorial can be applied to any Python web scraper built with common libraries like BeautifulSoup, Scrapy, <a class="!underline" href="/blog/browser-automation-tools-comparison">Selenium</a>, or <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">lxml</code>. If you want to follow along with a scraper of your own, make sure to have it in a script and ready to go.</p>
<div class="!text-title-h5 [&amp;:not(:first-child)]:mt-72 mb-24 relative"><span class="absolute -top-[120px]" id="prerequisites"></span><h2 class="!text-title-h5">Prerequisites</h2></div>
<p>This article assumes that you are already comfortable with web scraping and its related concepts like HTML structure, CSS selectors, HTTP requests, and handling rate limits. If you need a refresher on web scraping basics, check out <a class="!underline" href="https://realpython.com/python-web-scraping-practical-introduction/">this introductory guide to web scraping with Python</a>.</p>
<p>Otherwise, let&#x27;s jump in by setting up the tools we will use for the tutorial.</p>
<div class="blog-heading relative [&amp;:not(:first-child)]:mt-48 mb-16"><span class="absolute -top-[120px]" id="environment-setup"></span><h3>Environment setup</h3></div>
<p>We will mainly use Firecrawl in this article, so, please make sure that you sign up at <a class="!underline" href="https://www.firecrawl.dev">firecrawl.dev</a>, choose the free plan and get an API token.</p>
<p>Then, create a new working directory on your machine to follow along in this tutorial:</p>

<p>It is always a best practice to create a new virtual environment for projects. In this tutorial, we will use Python&#x27;s <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">virtualenv</code>:</p>

<p>Now, let&#x27;s install the libraries we will use:</p>
<figure data-rehype-pretty-code-figure=""><div class="relative"><div class="absolute top-0 left-[calc(50%-50vw)] z-[1] w-screen h-1 bg-border-faint"><div class="cmw-container absolute top-0 left-0 style="color:var(--shiki-token-string)"> beautifulsoup4</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string)"> firecrawl-py</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string)"> python-dotenv</span></span></code></pre></div></div></div></figure>
<p>Explore a project using Firecrawl: <a class="!underline" href="https://www.firecrawl.dev/blog/os-watch-github-monitoring-tool">Building an Open-Source Project Monitoring Tool with Firecrawl and Streamlit</a>. You might also like <a class="!underline" href="https://www.firecrawl.dev/blog/contradiction-agent">Build an agent that checks for website contradictions</a>.</p>
<p>We will touch on what each library does as we use them.</p>
<div class="blog-heading relative [&amp;:not(:first-child)]:mt-48 mb-16"><span class="absolute -top-[120px]" id="firecrawl-api-key-setup"></span><h3>Firecrawl API key setup</h3></div>
<p>Since we will push our code to a GitHub repository later, you will need to save your Firecrawl API key securely by using a <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">.env</code> file:</p>
<figure data-rehype-pretty-code-figure=""><div class="relative"><div class="absolute top-0 left-[calc(50%-50vw)] z-[1] w-screen h-1 bg-border-faint"><div class="cmw-container absolute top-0 left-0 style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">touch</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string)"> .env</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">echo</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> &quot;FIRECRAWL_API_KEY=&#x27;your-key-here&#x27;&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)"> &gt;&gt;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string)"> .env</span></span></code></pre></div></div></div></figure>
<p>Also, create a <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">.gitignore</code> file and add the <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">.env</code> to it so that it isn&#x27;t pushed to GitHub:</p>
<figure data-rehype-pretty-code-figure=""><div class="relative"><div class="absolute top-0 left-[calc(50%-50vw)] z-[1] w-screen h-1 bg-border-faint"><div class="cmw-container absolute top-0 left-0 <div class="!text-title-h5 [&amp;:not(:first-child)]:mt-72 mb-24 relative"><span class="absolute -top-[120px]" id="writing-a-basic-web-scraper"></span><h2 class="!text-title-h5">Writing a Basic Web Scraper</h2></div>
<p>In this tutorial, we will build a scraper for <a class="!underline" href="https://news.ycombinator.com/">the Hacker News homepage</a> that extracts post title, URL, author, rank, number of upvotes and date.</p>
<p><img src="/images/blog/scheduling-scrapers-images/hacker_news_homepage.webp" alt="Hacker News Homepage showing posts to be scraped using automated scheduling with Firecrawl"/></p>
<p>Like we mentioned, we will build the scraper in Firecrawl but I have also prepared an <a class="!underline" href="https://github.com/firecrawl/firecrawl/tree/main/examples/blog-articles/scheduling_scrapers">identical scraper written in BeautifulSoup</a> if you want a more traditional approach.</p>
<p>In your working directory, create a new <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">firecrawl_scraper.py</code> script and import the following packages:</p>
<figure data-rehype-pretty-code-figure=""><div class="relative"><div class="absolute top-0 left-[calc(50%-50vw)] z-[1] w-screen h-1 bg-border-faint"><div class="cmw-container absolute top-0 left-0 pointer-events-none"><svg fill="none" height="21" viewBox="0 0 22 21" width="22" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="pointer-events-none contain-[layout,paint] absolute absolute -top-10 -left-[10.5px] z-[1]"><path d="M10.5 4C10.5 7.31371 7.81371 10 4.5 10H0.5V11H4.5C7.81371 11 10.5 13.6863 10.5 17V21H11.5V17C11.5 13.6863 14.1863 11 17.5 11H21.5V10H17.5C14.1863 10 11.5 7.31371 11.5 4V0H10.5V4Z" fill="var(--border-faint)"></path></svg><svg fill="none" height="21" viewBox="0 0 22 21" width="22" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="pointer-events-none contain-[layout,paint] -mx-32"><div class="border-x overlay border-border-faint"></div><svg fill="none" height="11" viewBox="0 0 21 11" width="21" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="pointer-events-none 3.68629 13.6863 1 17 1H21V0H0V1H4C7.31371 1 10 3.68629 10 7V11H11V7Z" fill="var(--border-faint)"></path></svg><svg fill="none" height="11" viewBox="0 0 21 11" width="21" contain-[layout,paint] absolute absolute bottom-0 -left-10 z-[1]"><path d="M11 4C11 7.31371 13.6863 10 17 10H21V11H0V10H4C7.31371 10 10 7.31371 10 4V0H11V4Z" fill="var(--border-faint)"></path></svg><div class="p-32 bg-black/1 relative overflow-x-auto overflow-y-auto max-h-400"><pre tabindex="0" data-language="python" data-theme="css-variables" class="font-mono text-mono-medium"><code data-language="python" data-theme="css-variables" style="display:grid" class="font-mono text-mono-medium"><span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-comment)"># firecrawl_scraper.py</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">import</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)"> json</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">from</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)"> firecrawl </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">import</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)"> FirecrawlApp</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">from</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)"> dotenv </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">import</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)"> load_dotenv</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">from</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)"> pydantic </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">import</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)"> BaseModel</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">,</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)"> Field</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">from</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)"> typing </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">import</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)"> List</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">from</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)"> datetime </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">import</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)"> datetime</span></span>
<span data-line=""> </span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">load_dotenv</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">()</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">BASE_URL </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">=</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> &quot;https://news.ycombinator.com/&quot;</span></span></code></pre></div></div></div></figure>
<p>After the imports, we are calling <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">load_dotenv()</code> so that our Firecrawl API key is loaded from the <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">.env</code> file. Then, we are defining a new variable containing the URL we will scrape.</p>
<p>Next, we create a Pydantic model to specify the information we want to scrape from each Hacker News post:</p>

class="font-mono text-mono-medium"><code data-language="python" data-theme="css-variables" style="display:grid" class="font-mono text-mono-medium"><span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">class</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)"> NewsItem</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">(</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">BaseModel</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">):</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">    title</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)"> str</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)"> =</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)"> Field</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">(description</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">=</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">&quot;The title of the news item&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">)</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">    source_url</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)"> str</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)"> =</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)"> Field</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">(description</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">=</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">&quot;The URL of the news item&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">)</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">    author</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)"> str</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)"> =</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)"> Field</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">(</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">        description</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">=</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">&quot;The URL of the post author&#x27;s profile concatenated with the base URL.&quot;</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">    )</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">    rank</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)"> str</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)"> =</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)"> Field</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">(description</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">=</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">&quot;The rank of the news item&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">)</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">    upvotes</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)"> str</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)"> =</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)"> Field</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">(description</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">=</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">&quot;The number of upvotes of the news item&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">)</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">    date</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)"> str</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)"> =</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)"> Field</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">(description</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">=</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">&quot;The date of the news item.&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">)</span></span></code></pre></div></div></div></figure>
<p>Pydantic models are Python classes that provide data validation and serialization capabilities. They allow you to define the structure and types of your data using Python type hints, while automatically handling validation, serialization, and documentation.</p>
<p>In the context of our Firecrawl scraper, the <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">NewsItem</code> model defines the exact structure of data we want to extract from each Hacker News post. Each field in the model (<code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">title</code>, <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">source_url</code>, <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">author</code>, etc.) specifies what data should be scraped and includes a description of what that field represents.</p>
<p>This model is crucial for Firecrawl because it uses the model&#x27;s schema to understand exactly what data to extract from the webpage. When we pass this model to Firecrawl, it will automatically attempt to find and extract data matching these field definitions from the HTML structure of Hacker News.</p>
<p>For example, when Firecrawl sees we want a &quot;title&quot; field, it will look for elements on the page that are likely to contain post titles based on their HTML structure and content. The <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">Field</code> descriptions help provide additional context about what each piece of data represents.</p>
<p>Next, we create another model called <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">NewsData</code> that contains a list of <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">NewsItem</code> objects. This model will serve as a container for all the news items we scrape from Hacker News. The <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">news_items</code> field is defined as a List of <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">NewsItem</code> objects, which means it can store multiple news items in a single data structure.</p>

<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">    news_items</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)"> List</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">[</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">NewsItem</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">]</span></span></code></pre></div></div></div></figure>
<p>Without this second model, our scraper will return not one but all news items.</p>
<p>Now, we define a new function that will run Firecrawl based on the scraping schema we just defined:</p>

<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">    app </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">=</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)"> FirecrawlApp</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">()</span></span>
<span data-line=""> </span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">    data </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">=</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)"> app</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">.</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">scrape_url</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">(</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">        BASE_URL,</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">        params</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">=</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">{</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">            &quot;formats&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">: [</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">&quot;extract&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">],</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">            &quot;extract&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">: {</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">&quot;schema&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">: NewsData.</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">model_json_schema</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">()},</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">        },</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">    )</span></span>
<span data-line=""> </span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">    return</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)"> data</span></span></code></pre></div></div></div></figure>
<p>This function initializes a FirecrawlApp instance and uses it to scrape data from Hacker News. It passes the <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">BASE_URL</code> and parameters specifying that we want to extract data according to our <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">NewsData</code> schema. The schema tells Firecrawl exactly what fields to look for and extract from each news item on the page. The function returns the scraped data which will contain a list of news items matching our defined structure.</p>
<p>Let&#x27;s quickly test it:</p>

class="font-mono text-mono-medium"><code data-language="python" data-theme="css-variables" style="display:grid" class="font-mono text-mono-medium"><span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">{</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">    &#x27;title&#x27;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> &#x27;Hacker News&#x27;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">,</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">    &#x27;language&#x27;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> &#x27;en&#x27;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">,</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">    &#x27;ogLocaleAlternate&#x27;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)"> []</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">,</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">    &#x27;referrer&#x27;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> &#x27;origin&#x27;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">,</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">    &#x27;viewport&#x27;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> &#x27;width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0&#x27;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">,</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">    &#x27;sourceURL&#x27;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> &#x27;https://news.ycombinator.com/&#x27;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">,</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">    &#x27;url&#x27;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> &#x27;https://news.ycombinator.com/&#x27;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">,</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">    &#x27;statusCode&#x27;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)"> 200</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">}</span></span></code></pre></div></div></div></figure>
<p>The first key is the metadata field containing basic page information. We are interested in the <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">extract</code> field which contains the data scraped by the engine:</p>
<figure data-rehype-pretty-code-figure=""><div class="relative"><div class="absolute top-0 left-[calc(50%-50vw)] z-[1] w-screen h-1 bg-border-faint"><div class="cmw-container absolute top-0 left-0 <span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">    &#x27;title&#x27;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> &quot;Send someone you appreciate an official &#x27;Continue and Persist&#x27; Letter&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">,</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">    &#x27;source_url&#x27;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> &#x27;https://ContinueAndPersist.org&#x27;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">,</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">    &#x27;author&#x27;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> &#x27;https://news.ycombinator.com/user?id=adnanaga&#x27;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">,</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">    &#x27;rank&#x27;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> &#x27;1&#x27;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">,</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">    &#x27;upvotes&#x27;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> &#x27;1122&#x27;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">,</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">    &#x27;date&#x27;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> &#x27;17 hours ago&#x27;</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">}</span></span></code></pre></div></div></div></figure>
<p>The <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">extract</code> field contains a dictionary with a list of scraped news items. We can see above that when printing the first item, it includes all the fields we defined in our <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">NewsItem</code> Pydantic model, including title, source URL, author, rank, upvotes and date.</p>

<p>The output shows 30 news items, confirming that our scraper successfully extracted all posts from the first page of Hacker News. This matches the site&#x27;s standard layout which displays exactly 30 posts per page.</p>
<p>Now, let&#x27;s create a new function that saves this data to a JSON file:</p>

fill="var(--border-faint)"></path></svg><div class="p-32 bg-black/1 relative overflow-x-auto overflow-y-auto max-h-400"><pre tabindex="0" data-language="python" data-theme="css-variables" class="font-mono text-mono-medium"><code data-language="python" data-theme="css-variables" style="display:grid" class="font-mono text-mono-medium"><span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">def</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)"> save_firecrawl_news_data</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">():</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-comment)">    # Get the data</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">    data </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">=</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)"> get_firecrawl_news_data</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">()</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-comment)">    # Format current date for filename</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">    date_str </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">=</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)"> datetime</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">.</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">now</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">().</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">strftime</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">(</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">&quot;%Y_%m_</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)">%d</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">_%H_%M&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">)</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">    filename </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">=</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)"> f</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">&quot;firecrawl_hacker_news_data_</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)">{</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">date_str</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)">}</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">.json&quot;</span></span>
<span data-line=""> </span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-comment)">    # Save the news items to JSON file</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">    with</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)"> open</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">(filename, </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">&quot;w&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">)</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)"> as</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)"> f</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">:</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">        json</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">.</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">dump</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">(data[</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">&quot;extract&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">][</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">&quot;news_items&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">], f, indent</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">=</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)">4</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">)</span></span>
<span data-line=""> </span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">    return</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)"> filename</span></span></code></pre></div></div></div></figure>
<p><code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">save_firecrawl_news_data()</code> handles saving the scraped Hacker News data to a JSON file. It first calls <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">get_firecrawl_news_data()</code> to fetch the latest data from Hacker News. Then, it generates a filename using the current timestamp in the format <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">YYYY_MM_DD_HH_MM</code>. The data is saved to this timestamped JSON file with proper indentation, and the filename is returned. This allows us to maintain a historical record of the scraped data with clear timestamps indicating when each scrape occurred.</p>
<p>Finally, add a <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">__main__</code> block to the <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">firecrawl_scraper.py</code> script to allow running the scraper directly from the command line:</p>

style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> &quot;__main__&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">:</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">    save_firecrawl_news_data</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">()</span></span></code></pre></div></div></div></figure>
<p>The complete scraper script is available in <a class="!underline" href="https://github.com/firecrawl/firecrawl/blob/main/examples/hacker_news_scraper/firecrawl_scraper.py">our GitHub repository</a>. For reference, we also provide <a class="!underline" href="https://github.com/firecrawl/firecrawl/blob/main/examples/hacker_news_scraper/bs4_scraper.py">a BeautifulSoup implementation of the same scraper</a>.</p>
<div class="!text-title-h5 [&amp;:not(:first-child)]:mt-72 mb-24 relative"><span class="absolute -top-[120px]" id="local-web-scraping-automation-methods"></span><h2 class="!text-title-h5">Local Web Scraping Automation Methods</h2></div>
<p>In this section, we will explore how to run the scraper from the previous section on schedule using local tools like the Python <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">schedule</code> library and cron.</p>
<div class="blog-heading relative [&amp;:not(:first-child)]:mt-48 mb-16"><span class="absolute -top-[120px]" id="the-basics-of-the-python-,[object-object],-library"></span><h3>The basics of the Python <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">schedule</code> library</h3></div>
<p><code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">schedule</code> is a job scheduling library written for humans (<a class="!underline" href="https://schedule.readthedocs.io/en/stable/index.html">from the documentation</a>). It runs any Python function or callable periodically using intuitive syntax.</p>
<p>To get started, please install it with <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">pip</code>:</p>
<figure data-rehype-pretty-code-figure=""><div class="relative"><div class="absolute top-0 left-[calc(50%-50vw)] z-[1] w-screen h-1 bg-border-faint"><div class="cmw-container absolute top-0 left-0 <p>Then, scheduling a Python function is as easy as shown in the codeblock below:</p>

<p>To implement scheduling, first convert your task into a function (which we&#x27;ve already completed). Next, apply scheduling logic using the <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">.every(n).period.do</code> syntax. Below are several examples demonstrating different scheduling patterns:</p>

<p>There is much more to the <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">schedule</code> library than what we just covered (you should check out <a class="!underline" href="https://schedule.readthedocs.io/en/stable/examples.html">the examples from the documentation</a>) but they are enough for the purposes of this article.</p>
<div class="blog-heading relative [&amp;:not(:first-child)]:mt-48 mb-16"><span class="absolute -top-[120px]" id="using-python&#x27;s-,[object-object],-library-to-schedule-web-scrapers"></span><h3>Using Python&#x27;s <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">schedule</code> library to schedule web scrapers</h3></div>
<p>Now that we know the basics of <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">schedule</code>, let&#x27;s use it for our Firecrawl scraper. Start by creating a new <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">scrape_scheduler.py</code> script and making the necessary imports:</p>
<figure data-rehype-pretty-code-figure=""><div class="relative"><div class="absolute top-0 left-[calc(50%-50vw)] z-[1] w-screen h-1 bg-border-faint"><div class="cmw-container absolute top-0 left-0 <span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">from</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)"> firecrawl_scraper </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">import</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)"> save_firecrawl_news_data</span></span></code></pre></div></div></div></figure>
<p>Here, we import the <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">schedule</code> module itself and the <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">save_firecrawl_news_data()</code> function from <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">firecrawl_scraper.py</code> that downloads the top 30 posts of Hacker News.</p>
<p>Then, to run this function on schedule, like every hour, we only need to add a few lines of code:</p>

<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">schedule</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">.</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">every</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">().</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">hour</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">.</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">do</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">(save_firecrawl_news_data)</span></span>
<span data-line=""> </span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">while</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)"> True</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">:</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">    schedule</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">.</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">run_pending</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">()</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">    time</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">.</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">sleep</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">(</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)">1</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">)</span></span></code></pre></div></div></div></figure>
<p>You can start the schedule with:</p>

style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">python</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string)"> scrape_scheduler.py</span></span></code></pre></div></div></div></figure>
<blockquote>
<p><strong>Tip</strong>: For debugging purposes, start with a shorter interval like 60 seconds before implementing the hourly schedule.</p>
</blockquote>
<p>The scheduler will continue running until you terminate the main terminal process executing the <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">scrape_scheduler.py</code> script. Thanks to Firecrawl&#x27;s AI-powered HTML parsing and layout adaptation capabilities, the scraper is quite resilient to website changes and has a low probability of breaking.</p>
<p>Nevertheless, web scraping can be unpredictable, so it&#x27;s recommended to review <a class="!underline" href="https://schedule.readthedocs.io/en/stable/exception-handling.html">the exception handling</a> section of the <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">schedule</code> documentation to handle potential errors gracefully.</p>
<hr/>
<p>The <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">schedule</code> library provides a simple and intuitive way to run periodic tasks like web scrapers. While it lacks some advanced features of other scheduling methods, it&#x27;s a great choice for basic scheduling needs and getting started with automated scraping. Just remember to implement proper error handling for production use.</p>
<div class="blog-heading relative [&amp;:not(:first-child)]:mt-48 mb-16"><span class="absolute -top-[120px]" id="using-python&#x27;s-built-in-tools-to-automate-web-scrapers"></span><h3>Using Python&#x27;s built-in tools to automate web scrapers</h3></div>
<p>In this section, we will explore a few other local scheduling methods that have the advantage of being built into Python or the operating system, making them more reliable and robust than third-party libraries. These methods also provide better error handling, logging capabilities, and system-level control over the scheduling process.</p>
<div class="blog-heading relative [&amp;:not(:first-child)]:mt-32 mb-8"><span class="absolute -top-[120px]" id="how-to-automate-a-web-scraper-with-,[object-object],?"></span><h4>How to automate a web scraper with <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">asyncio</code>?</h4></div>
<p><code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">asyncio</code> is a Python library for running code concurrently - executing multiple tasks at the same time by switching between them when one is waiting. It&#x27;s built into Python and helps schedule tasks efficiently. Here&#x27;s why it&#x27;s great for web scrapers:</p>
<ol>
<li>It can do other work while waiting for web requests to complete.</li>
<li>You can run multiple scrapers at the same time with precise timing control.</li>
<li>It uses less computer resources than regular multitasking.</li>
<li>It handles errors well with <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">try/except</code> blocks.</li>
</ol>
<p>Let&#x27;s see how to use <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">asyncio</code> for scheduling scrapers:</p>
<figure data-rehype-pretty-code-figure=""><div class="relative"><div class="absolute top-0 left-[calc(50%-50vw)] z-[1] w-screen h-1 bg-border-faint"><div class="cmw-container absolute top-0 left-0 pointer-events-none"><svg fill="none" height="21" viewBox="0 0 22 21" width="22" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="pointer-events-none contain-[layout,paint] absolute absolute -top-10 -left-[10.5px] z-[1]"><path d="M10.5 4C10.5 7.31371 7.81371 10 4.5 10H0.5V11H4.5C7.81371 11 10.5 13.6863 10.5 17V21H11.5V17C11.5 13.6863 14.1863 11 17.5 11H21.5V10H17.5C14.1863 10 11.5 7.31371 11.5 4V0H10.5V4Z" fill="var(--border-faint)"></path></svg><svg fill="none" height="21" viewBox="0 0 22 21" width="22" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="pointer-events-none contain-[layout,paint] <span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-comment)">            # Run the scraper</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">            filename </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">=</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)"> save_firecrawl_news_data</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">()</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">            print</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">(</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">f</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">&quot;Data saved to </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)">{</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">filename</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)">}</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">)</span></span>
<span data-line=""> </span>

<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">            print</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">(</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">f</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">&quot;Error during scraping: </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)">{</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">e</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)">}</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">)</span></span>
<span data-line=""> </span>

<span data-line=""> </span>
<span data-line=""> </span>

<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-comment)">    # Create tasks for different scheduling intervals</span></span>

<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">        schedule_scraper</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">(interval_hours</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">=</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)">1</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">),</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-comment)">  # Run every hour</span></span>

<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">    ]</span></span>
<span data-line=""> </span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-comment)">    # Run all tasks concurrently</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">    await</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)"> asyncio</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">.</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">gather</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">(</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">*</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">tasks)</span></span>
<span data-line=""> </span>
<span data-line=""> </span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">if</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)"> __name__</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)"> ==</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> &quot;__main__&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">:</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-comment)">    # Run the async scheduler</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">    asyncio</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">.</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">run</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">(</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">main</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">())</span></span></code></pre></div></div></div></figure>
<p>Let&#x27;s break down what&#x27;s happening above:</p>
<p>The <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">schedule_scraper()</code> function is an <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">async</code> function that runs indefinitely in a loop. For each iteration, it:</p>
<ol>
<li>Runs the scraper and saves the data</li>
<li>Handles any errors that occur during scraping</li>
<li>Waits for the specified interval using <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">asyncio.sleep()</code></li>
</ol>
<p>The <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">main()</code> function sets up concurrent execution of multiple scraper tasks with different intervals. This allows running multiple scrapers simultaneously without blocking each other.</p>
<p>This asyncio-based approach has several advantages over the <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">schedule</code> library:</p>
<ol>
<li>
<p>True concurrency: Multiple scrapers can run simultaneously without blocking each other, unlike <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">schedule</code> which runs tasks sequentially.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Precise Timing: <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">asyncio.sleep()</code> provides more accurate timing control compared to schedule&#x27;s <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">run_pending()</code> approach.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Resource Efficiency: <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">asyncio</code> uses cooperative multitasking which requires less system resources than <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">schedule</code>&#x27;s threading-based approach.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Better Error Handling: <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">Async/await</code> makes it easier to implement proper error handling and recovery</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Flexibility: You can easily add or remove scraper tasks and modify their intervals without affecting other tasks</p>
</li>
</ol>
<p>The code structure also makes it simple to extend functionality by adding more concurrent tasks or implementing additional error handling logic.</p>
<div class="blog-heading relative [&amp;:not(:first-child)]:mt-32 mb-8"><span class="absolute -top-[120px]" id="how-to-automate-a-web-scraper-with-cron-jobs?"></span><h4>How to automate a web scraper with cron jobs?</h4></div>
<p>Cron is a time-based job scheduler in Unix-like operating systems (Linux, macOS). Think of it as a digital scheduler or calendar that can automatically run programs at specified times. A cron job is simply a task that you schedule to run at specific intervals.</p>
<p>For web scraping, cron jobs are incredibly useful because they let you automate your scraper to run at predetermined times. For example, you could set up a cron job to:</p>
<ul>
<li>Run your scraper every hour to collect real-time data</li>
<li>Execute scraping tasks during off-peak hours (like 2 AM) to minimize server load</li>
<li>Collect data at specific times when websites update their content</li>
</ul>
<p>The scheduling format uses five time fields: minute, hour, day of month, month, and day of week. For instance:</p>
<ul>
<li><code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">0 * * * *</code> means &quot;run every hour at minute 0&quot;</li>
<li><code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">0 0 * * *</code> means &quot;run at midnight every day&quot;</li>
<li><code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">*/15 * * * *</code> means &quot;run every 15 minutes&quot;</li>
</ul>
<p>Cron jobs are especially reliable for web scraping because they&#x27;re built into the operating system, use minimal resources, and continue running even after system reboots.</p>
<p>So, let&#x27;s run the <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">save_firecrawl_news_data()</code> function on a schedule using cron. First, we will create a dedicated script for the cron job named <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">cron_scraper.py</code>:</p>
<figure data-rehype-pretty-code-figure=""><div class="relative"><div class="absolute top-0 left-[calc(50%-50vw)] z-[1] w-screen h-1 bg-border-faint"><div class="cmw-container absolute top-0 left-0 <span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">    format</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">=</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)">%(asctime)s</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> - </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)">%(levelname)s</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> - </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)">%(message)s</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">,</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">    handlers</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">=</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">[logging.</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">FileHandler</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">(log_file), logging.</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">StreamHandler</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">(sys.stdout)],</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">)</span></span>
<span data-line=""> </span>
<span data-line=""> </span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">def</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)"> main</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">():</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">    try</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">:</span></span>

<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">        filename </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">=</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)"> save_firecrawl_news_data</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">()</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-comment)">  # Actual scraping function</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">        logging</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">.</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">info</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">(</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">f</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">&quot;Successfully saved data to </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)">{</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">filename</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)">}</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">)</span></span>

<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">        logging</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">.</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">error</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">(</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">f</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">&quot;Scraping failed: </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)">{str</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">(e)</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)">}</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">, exc_info</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">=</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)">True</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">)</span></span>
<span data-line=""> </span>
<span data-line=""> </span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">if</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)"> __name__</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)"> ==</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> &quot;__main__&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">:</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">    main</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">()</span></span></code></pre></div></div></div></figure>
<p>The script implements a production-ready web scraper with logging and error handling. It creates a logs directory, configures detailed logging to both files and console, and wraps the scraping operation in error handling. When run, it executes our scraper function while tracking its progress and any potential issues, making it ideal for automated scheduling through cron jobs.</p>
<p>Now, to schedule this script using cron, you will need to make it executable:</p>

<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-comment)"># Run every hour</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">*/</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)">1</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)"> *</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)"> *</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)"> *</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)"> *</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)"> cd </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">/</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">absolute</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">/</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">path</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">/</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">to</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">/</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">project &amp;&amp; </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">/</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">absolute</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">/</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">path</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">/</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">to</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">/</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">.</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">venv</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">/</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">bin</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">/</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">python cron_scraper</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">.</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">py </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">&gt;&gt;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)"> ~/</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">cron</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">.</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">log </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)">2</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">&gt;&amp;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)">1</span></span></code></pre></div></div></div></figure>
<p>The above cron job syntax consists of several parts:</p>
<p>The timing pattern &quot;<em>/1</em> ** *&quot; breaks down as follows:</p>
<ul>
<li>First <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">*/1</code>: Specifies every minute</li>
<li>First <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">*</code>: Represents any hour</li>
<li>Second <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">*</code>: Represents any day of the month</li>
<li>Third <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">*</code>: Represents any month</li>
<li>Fourth <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">*</code>: Represents any day of the week</li>
</ul>
<p>After the timing pattern:</p>
<ul>
<li><code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">cd /absolute/path/to/project</code>: Changes to the project directory of your scraper</li>
<li><code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">&amp;&amp;</code>: Chains commands, executing the next only if previous succeeds</li>
<li><code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">/absolute/path/to/.venv/bin/python</code>: Specifies the Python interpreter path</li>
<li><code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">cron_scraper.py</code>: The script to execute</li>
<li><code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">&gt;&gt; ~/cron.log 2&gt;&amp;1</code>: Redirects both standard output (&gt;&gt;) and errors (<code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">2&gt;&amp;1</code>) to <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">cron.log</code></li>
</ul>
<p>For hourly execution, the same pattern applies but with <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">0 * * * *</code> timing to run at the start of each hour instead of every minute.</p>
<p>As soon as you save your crontab file with these commands, the schedule starts and you should see a <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">logs</code> directory in the same folder as your <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">cron_scraper.py</code>. It must look like this if you have been following along:</p>
<p><img src="/images/blog/scheduling-scrapers-images/output.png" alt="&quot;Logs directory showing successful automated web scraping schedule execution with cron jobs and Firecrawl&quot;"/></p>
<p>You can always check the status of your cron jobs with the following command as well:</p>
<figure data-rehype-pretty-code-figure=""><div class="relative"><div class="absolute top-0 left-[calc(50%-50vw)] z-[1] w-screen h-1 bg-border-faint"><div class="cmw-container absolute top-0 left-0 ~/cron.log</span></span></code></pre></div></div></div></figure>
<p>To cancel a cron job, simply open your crontab file again and remove the line corresponding to the job.</p>
<div class="blog-heading relative [&amp;:not(:first-child)]:mt-32 mb-8"><span class="absolute -top-[120px]" id="how-to-automate-a-web-scraper-using-windows-task-scheduler?"></span><h4>How to automate a web scraper using Windows Task Scheduler?</h4></div>
<p>Windows Task Scheduler is a built-in Windows tool that can automate running programs or scripts at specified times. It&#x27;s a robust alternative to cron jobs for Windows users. Let&#x27;s set up our scraper to run automatically.</p>
<p>First, create a batch file (<code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">run_scraper.bat</code>) to run our Python script:</p>
<figure data-rehype-pretty-code-figure=""><div class="relative"><div class="absolute top-0 left-[calc(50%-50vw)] z-[1] w-screen h-1 bg-border-faint"><div class="cmw-container absolute top-0 left-0 pointer-events-none"><svg fill="none" height="21" viewBox="0 0 22 21" width="22" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="pointer-events-none contain-[layout,paint] absolute absolute -top-10 -left-[10.5px] z-[1]"><path d="M10.5 4C10.5 7.31371 7.81371 10 4.5 10H0.5V11H4.5C7.81371 11 10.5 13.6863 10.5 17V21H11.5V17C11.5 13.6863 14.1863 11 17.5 11H21.5V10H17.5C14.1863 10 11.5 7.31371 11.5 4V0H10.5V4Z" fill="var(--border-faint)"></path></svg><svg fill="none" height="21" viewBox="0 0 22 21" width="22" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="pointer-events-none contain-[layout,paint] 11H21.5V10H17.5C14.1863 10 11.5 7.31371 11.5 4V0H10.5V4Z" fill="var(--border-faint)"></path></svg></div></div><div class="absolute bottom-0 left-[calc(50%-50vw)] z-[1] w-screen h-1 bg-border-faint"><div class="cmw-container absolute top-0 left-0 pointer-events-none"><svg fill="none" height="21" viewBox="0 0 22 21" width="22" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" -mx-32"><div class="border-x overlay border-border-faint"></div><svg fill="none" height="11" viewBox="0 0 21 11" width="21" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="pointer-events-none xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="pointer-events-none contain-[layout,paint] absolute absolute bottom-0 -right-10 z-[1]"><path d="M11 4C11 7.31371 13.6863 10 17 10H21V11H0V10H4C7.31371 10 10 7.31371 10 4V0H11V4Z" fill="var(--border-faint)"></path></svg><svg fill="none" height="11" viewBox="0 0 21 11" width="21" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="pointer-events-none fill="var(--border-faint)"></path></svg><div class="p-32 bg-black/1 relative overflow-x-auto overflow-y-auto max-h-400"><pre tabindex="0" data-language="python" data-theme="css-variables" class="font-mono text-mono-medium"><code data-language="python" data-theme="css-variables" style="display:grid" class="font-mono text-mono-medium"><span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)">@echo</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-function)"> off</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">cd </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">/</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">d </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)">&quot;C:\path\to\your\project&quot;</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">call venv</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">\</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">Scripts\activate</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">python cron_scraper</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-punctuation)">.</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">py</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">deactivate</span></span></code></pre></div></div></div></figure>
<p>Then, to set up the task in Windows Task Scheduler:</p>
<ul>
<li>Open Task Scheduler (search &quot;Task Scheduler&quot; in Windows search)</li>
<li>Click &quot;Create Basic Task&quot; in the right panel</li>
<li>Follow the wizard:<!-- -->
<ul>
<li>Name: &quot;Hacker News Scraper&quot;</li>
<li>Description: &quot;Scrapes Hacker News hourly&quot;</li>
<li>Trigger: Choose when to run (e.g., &quot;Daily&quot;)</li>
<li>Action: &quot;Start a program&quot;</li>
<li>Program/script: Browse to your <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">run_scraper.bat</code></li>
<li>Start in: Your project directory</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<p>For more control over the task, you can modify its properties after creation:</p>
<ul>
<li>Double-click the task</li>
<li>In the &quot;Triggers&quot; tab, click &quot;Edit&quot; to set custom schedules</li>
<li>Common scheduling options:<!-- -->
<ul>
<li>Run every hour</li>
<li>Run at specific times</li>
<li>Run on system startup</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>In the &quot;Settings&quot; tab, useful options include:<!-- -->
<ul>
<li>&quot;Run task as soon as possible after a scheduled start is missed&quot;</li>
<li>&quot;Stop the task if it runs longer than X hours&quot;</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<p>The Task Scheduler provides several advantages:</p>
<ul>
<li>Runs even when user is logged out</li>
<li>Detailed history and logging</li>
<li>Ability to run with elevated privileges</li>
<li>Options for network conditions</li>
<li>Retry logic for failed tasks</li>
</ul>
<p>You can monitor your scheduled task through the Task Scheduler interface or check the logs we set up in <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">cron_scraper.py</code>.</p>
<div class="!text-title-h5 [&amp;:not(:first-child)]:mt-72 mb-24 relative"><span class="absolute -top-[120px]" id="automating-web-scrapers-with-github-actions"></span><h2 class="!text-title-h5">Automating Web Scrapers With GitHub Actions</h2></div>
<p>One disadvantage all local scheduling methods have is that they rely on your local machine being powered on and connected to the internet. If your computer is turned off, loses power, or loses internet connectivity, your scheduled scraping tasks won&#x27;t run. This is where cloud-based solutions like GitHub Actions can provide more reliability and uptime for your web scraping workflows.</p>
<div class="blog-heading relative [&amp;:not(:first-child)]:mt-48 mb-16"><span class="absolute -top-[120px]" id="what-is-github-actions?"></span><h3>What is GitHub Actions?</h3></div>
<p>GitHub Actions is a continuous integration and deployment (CI/CD) platform provided by GitHub that allows you to automate various workflows directly from your <a class="!underline" href="https://github.com">GitHub</a> repository.</p>
<p>For web scraping, GitHub Actions provides a reliable way to schedule and run your scraping scripts in the cloud. You can define workflows using YAML files that specify when and how your scraper should run, such as on a regular schedule using cron syntax. This means your scraping jobs will continue running even when your local machine is off, as they execute on GitHub&#x27;s servers.</p>
<div class="blog-heading relative [&amp;:not(:first-child)]:mt-48 mb-16"><span class="absolute -top-[120px]" id="step-1:-setting-up-a-github-repository"></span><h3>Step 1: Setting Up a GitHub repository</h3></div>
<p>To schedule our scrapers with GitHub actions, we first need a GitHub repository. Start by initializing Git in your current workspace:</p>
<figure data-rehype-pretty-code-figure=""><div class="relative"><div class="absolute top-0 left-[calc(50%-50vw)] z-[1] w-screen h-1 bg-border-faint"><div class="cmw-container absolute top-0 left-0 xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="pointer-events-none contain-[layout,paint] absolute absolute bottom-0 -right-10 z-[1]"><path d="M11 4C11 7.31371 13.6863 10 17 10H21V11H0V10H4C7.31371 10 10 7.31371 10 4V0H11V4Z" fill="var(--border-faint)"></path></svg><svg fill="none" height="11" viewBox="0 0 21 11" width="21" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="pointer-events-none contain-[layout,paint] absolute absolute bottom-0 -left-10 z-[1]"><path d="M11 4C11 7.31371 13.6863 10 17 10H21V11H0V10H4C7.31371 10 10 7.31371 10 4V0H11V4Z" fill="var(--border-faint)"></path></svg><div class="p-32 bg-black/1 relative overflow-x-auto overflow-y-auto max-h-400"><pre tabindex="0" data-language="bash" data-theme="css-variables" class="font-mono text-mono-medium"><code data-language="bash" data-theme="css-variables" style="display:grid" class="font-mono text-mono-medium"><span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-comment)"># Initialize git in your project directory</span></span>

.github/workflows</span></span></code></pre></div></div></div></figure>
<p>This directory will contain our GitHub Actions workflow files with YAML format. These files define how and when our scraping scripts should run. The workflows can be scheduled using cron syntax, triggered by events like pushes or pull requests, and can include multiple steps like installing dependencies and running scripts.</p>
<div class="blog-heading relative [&amp;:not(:first-child)]:mt-48 mb-16"><span class="absolute -top-[120px]" id="step-2:-creating-a-workflow-file"></span><h3>Step 2: Creating a Workflow file</h3></div>
<p>At this stage, create a new <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">scraper.yml</code> file inside <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">.github/workflows</code> and paste the following contents:</p>
<figure data-rehype-pretty-code-figure=""><div class="relative"><div class="absolute top-0 left-[calc(50%-50vw)] z-[1] w-screen h-1 bg-border-faint"><div class="cmw-container absolute top-0 left-0 class="font-mono text-mono-medium"><code data-language="yaml" data-theme="css-variables" style="display:grid" class="font-mono text-mono-medium"><span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">name</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> Run Firecrawl Scraper</span></span>
<span data-line=""> </span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-constant)">on</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">:</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">  schedule</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">:</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">    - </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">cron</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> &quot;0/5 * * * *&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-comment)"> # Runs every five minute</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">  workflow_dispatch</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-comment)"> # Allows manual trigger</span></span>
<span data-line=""> </span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">jobs</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">:</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">  scrape</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">:</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">    runs-on</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> ubuntu-latest</span></span>
<span data-line=""> </span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">    steps</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">:</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">      - </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">uses</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> actions/checkout@v3</span></span>
<span data-line=""> </span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">      - </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">name</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> Set up Python</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">        uses</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> actions/setup-python@v4</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">        with</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">:</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">          python-version</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> &quot;3.9&quot;</span></span>
<span data-line=""> </span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">      - </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">name</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> Install dependencies</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">        run</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)"> |</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string)">          python -m pip install --upgrade pip</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string)">          pip install pydantic firecrawl-py</span></span>
<span data-line=""> </span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">      - </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">name</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> Run scraper</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">        run</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> python firecrawl_scraper.py</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">        env</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">:</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-comment)">          # Add any environment variables your scraper needs</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">          FIRECRAWL_API_KEY</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> ${{ secrets.FIRECRAWL_API_KEY }}</span></span>
<span data-line=""> </span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-foreground)">      - </span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">name</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string-expression)"> Commit and push if changes</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">        run</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)">:</span><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-keyword)"> |</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string)">          git config --global user.name &#x27;GitHub Actions Bot&#x27;</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string)">          git config --global user.email &#x27;actions@github.com&#x27;</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string)">          git add .</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string)">          git commit -m &quot;Update scraped data&quot; || exit 0</span></span>
<span data-line=""><span style="color:var(--shiki-token-string)">          git push</span></span></code></pre></div></div></div></figure>
<p>Let&#x27;s break down the key components of this GitHub Actions workflow file:</p>
<p>Workflow name:
The workflow is named &quot;Run Firecrawl Scraper&quot; which helps identify it in the GitHub Actions interface.</p>
<p>Triggers:</p>
<ul>
<li>Scheduled to run every 5 minutes using cron syntax <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">0/5 * * * *</code> (5 minutes is for debugging purposes, please change to hourly later)</li>
<li>Can be manually triggered using <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">workflow_dispatch</code></li>
</ul>
<p>Job configuration:</p>
<ul>
<li>Runs on latest Ubuntu virtual environment</li>
<li>Contains multiple sequential steps</li>
</ul>
<p>Step details:</p>
<ol>
<li>Checkout:<!-- -->
<ul>
<li>Uses <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">actions/checkout@v3</code> to get repository code</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>Python Setup:<!-- -->
<ul>
<li>Uses <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">actions/setup-python@v4</code></li>
<li>Configures Python 3.9 environment</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>Dependencies:<!-- -->
<ul>
<li>Upgrades <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">pip</code></li>
<li>Installs required packages: <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">pydantic</code> and <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">firecrawl-py</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>Scraper Execution:<!-- -->
<ul>
<li>Runs <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">firecrawl_scraper.py</code></li>
<li>Uses <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">FIRECRAWL_API_KEY</code> from repository secrets</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>Committing the changes:<!-- -->
<ul>
<li>Creates a commit persisting the downloaded data using GitHub Actions bot.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ol>
<p>To run this action successfully, you&#x27;ll need to store your Firecrawl API key in GitHub secrets. Navigate to your repository&#x27;s Settings, then Secrets and variables → Actions. Click the &quot;New repository secret&quot; button and add your API key, making sure to use the exact key name specified in <code class="font-mono text-mono-medium">scraper.yml</code>.</p>
<p>After ensuring that everything is set up correctly, commit and push the latest changes to GitHub:</p>
<figure data-rehype-pretty-code-figure=""><div class="relative"><div class="absolute top-0 left-[calc(50%-50vw)] z-[1] w-screen h-1 bg-border-faint"><div class="cmw-container absolute top-0 left-0 -mx-32"><div class="border-x overlay border-border-faint"></div><svg fill="none" height="11" viewBox="0 0 21 11" width="21" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="pointer-events-none 3.68629 13.6863 1 17 1H21V0H0V1H4C7.31371 1 10 3.68629 10 7V11H11V7Z" fill="var(--border-faint)"></path></svg><svg fill="none" height="11" viewBox="0 0 21 11" width="21"
