[txtai](https://github.com/neuml/txtai) - ⭐️10.7k\n\n![txtai logo showing a stylized text and AI representation for semantic search and embeddings database](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/neuml/txtai/master/logo.png)\n\ntxtai is an all-in-one open-source embeddings database designed for building comprehensive semantic search and language model workflows. Unlike frameworks that focus solely on retrieval or generation, txtai provides a complete ecosystem for RAG implementations by combining vector storage, text processing pipelines, and LLM orchestration in a single coherent package. Its streamlined API makes it particularly suitable for developers seeking to build production-ready RAG applications without the complexity of integrating multiple separate tools.\n\ntxtai offers a comprehensive set of features that make it versatile for RAG applications:\n\n- **Embeddings database** - Store, index, and search text and documents with semantic understanding\n- **Pipeline components** - Access pre-built components for summarization, translation, transcription, and more\n- **LLM integration** - Work with various language models for text generation and completion\n- **Workflow orchestration** - Chain components together into complex NLP workflows\n- **Multimodal support** - Process and analyze text, images, and audio in unified pipelines\n- **API and service layer** - Deploy as a REST API service with minimal configuration\n- **Containerization** - Run in Docker with provided configurations for scalability\n- **Cross-platform compatibility** - Work across different operating systems and environments\n\nGetting started with txtai is straightforward with installation via `pip install txtai`. The framework provides extensive documentation and examples, including a dedicated notebook for [building RAG pipelines](https://github.com/neuml/txtai/blob/master/examples/52_Build_RAG_pipelines_with_txtai.ipynb). The example demonstrates how to create embeddings, index documents, and build complete RAG workflows that combine retrieval with language model generation. txtai also offers a recommended model guide to help users select appropriate models for different components based on performance and licensing considerations.\n\n### 12. [RAGAS](https://github.com/explodinggradients/ragas) - ⭐️8.7k\n\n![RAGAS logo showing a stylized evaluation framework for retrieval-augmented generation systems](https://github.com/explodinggradients/ragas/raw/main/docs/_static/imgs/logo.png)\n\nRAGAS is a comprehensive evaluation toolkit designed specifically for assessing and optimizing RAG applications. Unlike frameworks focused on building RAG systems, RAGAS provides objective metrics and intelligent test generation capabilities that help developers measure the effectiveness of their retrieval and generation components. Its primary advantage lies in creating data-driven feedback loops that enable continuous improvement of LLM applications through rigorous evaluation.\n\nRAGAS offers a powerful set of features for evaluation:\n\n- **Objective metrics** - Evaluate RAG applications with precision using both LLM-based and traditional metrics\n- **Test data generation** - Automatically create comprehensive test datasets covering diverse scenarios\n- **Seamless integrations** - Works with popular LLM frameworks like LangChain and major [LLM observability tools](/blog/best-llm-observability-tools)\n- **Analytics dashboard** - Visualize and analyze evaluation results through app.ragas.io\n- **Metric alignment** - Train metrics to match specific evaluation preferences with few-shot examples\n- **Specialized RAG metrics** - Evaluate context precision, recall, faithfulness, and response relevancy\n- **Multi-framework support** - Compatible with various LLM models and RAG implementations\n\nGetting started with RAGAS is simple via `pip install ragas`. Results can be analyzed through the [app.ragas.io](https://app.ragas.io) dashboard. For detailed guidance, see the [installation guide](https://docs.ragas.io/en/latest/getstarted/install/), [evaluation documentation](https://docs.ragas.io/en/latest/getstarted/evals/), and [test set generation](https://docs.ragas.io/en/latest/getstarted/rag_testset_generation/) resources.\n\n### 13. [R2R (RAG to Riches)](https://github.com/SciPhi-AI/R2R) - ⭐️6.3k\n\nR2R is an advanced AI retrieval system that brings production-ready features to Retrieval-Augmented Generation workflows through a comprehensive RESTful API. Unlike many RAG frameworks focused primarily on basic document retrieval, R2R incorporates agentic reasoning capabilities with its [Deep Research API](https://www.firecrawl.dev/blog/best-deep-research-apis) that can perform multi-step reasoning by fetching relevant data from both your knowledge base and external sources. This combination of traditional RAG with intelligent agent behavior makes it particularly powerful for complex query resolution requiring nuanced understanding.\n\nR2R offers an impressive array of features designed for production deployment:\n\n- **Multimodal ingestion** - Process diverse content formats including text, PDFs, images, and audio files\n- **Hybrid search capability** - Combine semantic and keyword search with reciprocal rank fusion for better relevancy\n- **Knowledge graph integration** - Automatically extract entities and relationships to build contextual knowledge graphs\n- **Agentic reasoning** - Leverage the Deep Research agent for complex, multi-step information gathering and synthesis\n- **Production infrastructure** - Includes user authentication, collection management, and comprehensive API access\n- **Multiple deployment options** - Available as cloud service or self-hosted solution with Docker support\n- **Client SDKs** - Provides both Python and JavaScript libraries for streamlined integration\n\nGetting started with R2R offers two primary paths: using [SciPhi Cloud](https://r2r-docs.sciphi.ai/introduction)'s managed deployment with a generous free tier (no credit card required) or [self-hosting](https://github.com/SciPhi-AI/R2R). For the quickest self-hosted setup, you can install via pip (`pip install r2r`), set your OpenAI API key, and run `python -m r2r.serve` for a lightweight deployment. Alternatively, Docker Compose provides a full-featured deployment with `docker compose -f compose.full.yaml --profile postgres up -d`. The [Python SDK](https://r2r-docs.sciphi.ai/api-sdks/python-sdk) offers intuitive methods for document operations, searches, and agentic RAG queries through a clean client interface.\n\n### 14. [Ragatouille](https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/RAGatouille) - ⭐️3.4k\n\n![Ragatouille logo showing a stylized RAG framework with late-interaction retrieval capabilities for enhanced document search accuracy](https://encrypted-tbn0.gstatic.com/images?q=tbn:ANd9GcS6vaDl6N-cTM4pamO93Ozl4qHJnV6Nffja2g\u0026s)\n\nRagatouille is a framework that implements late-interaction retrieval methods based on ColBERT for RAG applications. Unlike traditional dense retrieval that uses single vector representations, Ragatouille preserves token-level information during matching, resulting in higher retrieval accuracy. This approach bridges the gap between advanced information retrieval research and practical RAG implementations, delivering superior search quality without excessive computational requirements.\n\nRagatouille offers several key features for enhanced retrieval:\n\n- **Late-interaction retrieval** - Uses ColBERT's token-level matching for more precise document retrieval\n- **Fine-tuning capabilities** - Supports training on domain-specific data without explicit annotations\n- **Metadata support** - Maintains document metadata throughout indexing and retrieval\n- **Flexible document handling** - Provides utilities for document processing and management\n- **Multiple query processing** - Handles batched queries efficiently\n- **Disk-based indices** - Creates compressed indices that integrate easily with production systems\n- **Integration options** - Works with Vespa, Intel FastRAG, and LlamaIndex\n\nGetting started with Ragatouille requires a simple pip installation. The [documentation](https://ben.clavie.eu/ragatouille/) provides comprehensive guides for implementation, and the [examples directory](https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/RAGatouille/tree/main/examples) contains notebooks for various use cases. Particularly useful is the [fine-tuning without annotations example](https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/RAGatouille/blob/main/examples/03-finetuning_without_annotations_with_instructor_and_RAGatouille.ipynb), which demonstrates how to train domain-specific models using synthetic query generation with Instructor embeddings.\n\n### 15. [FlashRAG](https://github.com/RUC-NLPIR/FlashRAG) - ⭐️2.1k\n\nFlashRAG is a Python toolkit for Retrieval-Augmented Generation research that provides 36 pre-processed benchmark datasets and 17 state-of-the-art RAG algorithms in a unified interface. Unlike implementation-focused frameworks, FlashRAG prioritizes reproducibility and experimentation, enabling researchers to quickly reproduce existing works or develop new approaches without the overhead of data preparation and baseline implementation.\n\nFlashRAG provides several key features for research:\n\n- **Extensive dataset support** - Access to 36 pre-processed benchmark RAG datasets from question answering to entity linking\n- **Algorithm implementations** - 17 state-of-the-art RAG methods implemented with consistent interfaces\n- **Modular architecture** - Easily swap different retrievers, generators, and other components\n- **Web interface** - Intuitive UI for interactive experimentation and visualization\n- **Comprehensive documentation** - Detailed guidance for reproducing experiments\n- **Performance benchmarks** - Ready-to-use evaluation metrics and comparisons\n- **Multimodal capabilities** - Support for text, images, and other modalities in RAG pipelines\n\nGetting started with FlashRAG requires installing the package via pip and exploring its components. The toolkit provides examples in its GitHub repository for using different retrievers and building RAG pipelines. Pre-processed datasets are available through Hugging Face, and a web UI simplifies experimentation without writing code.\n\n## Decision Table For Choosing the Right RAG Framework\n\n| Framework   | Primary focus          | Best for                           | Key features                                                               | Deployment complexity | GitHub stars |\n| ----------- | ---------------------- | ---------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------- | ------------ |\n| LangChain   | Component chaining     | General RAG applications           | Data connections, model flexibility, integrations                          | Medium                | 105k         |\n| Dify        | Visual development     | Non-technical users, enterprise    | Visual workflow editor, extensive model support, agent capabilities        | Low (Docker)          | 90.5k        |\n| RAGFlow     | Document processing    | Complex document handling          | Deep document understanding, GraphRAG, visual interface                    | Medium                | 48.5k        |\n| LlamaIndex  | Data indexing          | Custom knowledge sources           | Flexible connectors, customizable indexing, modular architecture           | Low                   | 40.8k        |\n| Milvus      | Vector storage         | Large-scale vector search          | Advanced vector search, horizontal scalability, hybrid search              | Medium                | 33.9k        |\n| mem0        | Persistent memory      | Assistants with context retention  | Multi-level memory, automatic processing, dual storage                     | Low                   | 27.3k        |\n| DSPy        | Prompt optimization    | Systems requiring self-improvement | Modular architecture, automatic prompt optimization, evaluation            | Medium                | 23k          |\n| Haystack    | Pipeline orchestration | Production applications            | Flexible components, technology-agnostic, evaluation tools                 | Medium                | 20.2k        |\n| LightRAG    | Performance            | Speed-critical applications        | Simple architecture, information diversity, comprehensive retrieval        | Low                   | 14.6k        |\n| LLMWare     | Resource efficiency    | Edge/CPU deployment                | Efficient models, comprehensive processing, parallelized parsing           | Low                   | 12.7k        |\n| txtai       | All-in-one solution    | Streamlined implementation         | Embeddings database, pipeline components, multimodal support               | Low                   | 10.7k        |\n| RAGAS       | Evaluation             | RAG system testing                 | Objective metrics, test generation, analytics dashboard                    | Low                   | 8.7k         |\n| R2R         | Agent-based RAG        | Complex queries                    | Multimodal ingestion, agentic reasoning, knowledge graphs                  | Medium                | 6.3k         |\n| Ragatouille | Advanced retrieval     | High precision search              | Late-interaction retrieval, fine-tuning capabilities, token-level matching | Medium                | 3.4k         |\n| FlashRAG    | Research               | Experimentation, benchmarking      | Pre-processed datasets, algorithm implementations, web interface           | Medium                | 2.1k         |\n\n### Selection criteria\n\n- **Ease of implementation**: choose Dify, LlamaIndex, mem0, LightRAG, or txtai\n- **Document-heavy applications**: consider RAGFlow or LLMWare\n- **Production at scale**: look at Milvus, Haystack, or LangChain\n- **Limited hardware resources**: prioritize LLMWare or LightRAG\n- **Complex reasoning needs**: explore R2R or DSPy\n- **Evaluation focus**: use RAGAS\n- **Research purposes**: select FlashRAG\n\n## Conclusion\n\nThe landscape of RAG frameworks has evolved significantly in 2025, with solutions addressing different aspects of the RAG pipeline from document ingestion to retrieval, generation, and evaluation.\n\nWhen selecting a framework, consider your specific use case requirements, technical expertise, and deployment constraints. Some frameworks like LangChain and LlamaIndex offer comprehensive end-to-end solutions, while others like Ragatouille and FlashRAG excel in specific areas such as advanced retrieval techniques or research experimentation. Your choice should align with your application's scale, performance needs, and development timeline.\n\nIf you're evaluating enterprise solutions with built-in support and security features, see our comparison of [enterprise RAG platforms](/blog/best-enterprise-rag-platforms-2025).\n\nRegardless of which framework you choose, your RAG system's performance depends heavily on the quality and relevance of your knowledge base. This is where Firecrawl proves valuable, offering efficient web data collection that works with any RAG framework.\n\nWhether you need to generate complete text representations of websites, extract structured information using natural language, or add deep research capabilities, Firecrawl makes data collection straightforward. Explore [web scraping libraries](/blog/best-open-source-web-scraping-libraries) for additional data collection methods, learn about [browser automation tools](/blog/browser-automation-tools-comparison) for dynamic content extraction, or build visual workflows with our [LangFlow tutorial](/blog/langflow-tutorial-visual-ai-workflows).\n\nStart your RAG project by building a solid knowledge base with [Firecrawl](https://firecrawl.dev), then select the framework that best fits your technical requirements.\n\n## FAQs\n\n**What is RAG and why is it still relevant in 2026?**\n\nRAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) enhances LLMs by connecting them to external knowledge sources for more accurate, contextual responses. Despite larger context windows in models like Llama 4, RAG remains essential for most applications due to resource constraints and improved accuracy.\n\n**Which RAG framework is best for beginners?**\n\nDify, LlamaIndex, and txtai offer the lowest barriers to entry. Dify provides a visual interface requiring minimal coding, LlamaIndex has straightforward documentation and examples, while txtai delivers an all-in-one solution with simple installation and comprehensive features.\n\n**Can I use multiple RAG frameworks together?**\n\nYes, many frameworks are designed to work together. For example, you can use Milvus as your vector database with LangChain or LlamaIndex for orchestration, while adding RAGAS for evaluation. This modular approach lets you combine strengths of different tools.\n\n**How does Firecrawl integrate with RAG frameworks?**\n\nFirecrawl provides high-quality web data collection that works with any RAG framework. It converts websites to LLM-friendly formats, extracts structured information, and enables deep research capabilities. The collected data can then be indexed by your chosen RAG framework for retrieval.\n\n**What's the difference between vector databases and RAG frameworks?**\n\nVector databases like Milvus store and retrieve embedding vectors efficiently. RAG frameworks like LangChain or LlamaIndex provide the complete pipeline including document processing, embedding generation, retrieval orchestration, and LLM integration. Most RAG frameworks integrate with multiple vector databases.\n\n**How do I evaluate my RAG system's performance?**\n\nUse RAGAS for comprehensive evaluation with objective metrics measuring context precision, recall, faithfulness, and response relevancy. It also generates test datasets automatically. Most frameworks like LangChain and Haystack include built-in evaluation tools for measuring retrieval and generation quality.\n\n**What hardware requirements do RAG frameworks have?**\n\nRequirements vary significantly. LLMWare and LightRAG can run on standard CPUs and laptops. Frameworks like Milvus handling large-scale vector search benefit from GPUs. Cloud-based solutions like Dify and R2R's SciPhi Cloud eliminate local hardware concerns entirely.\n\n**Can RAG frameworks handle multimodal data?**\n\nYes, several frameworks support multimodal data. LlamaIndex, txtai, and R2R can process text, images, audio, and video. Milvus stores embeddings from various data types, while RAGFlow excels at extracting information from complex documents including tables and visual elements.\n\n**What's the difference between basic RAG and advanced techniques like GraphRAG?**\n\nBasic RAG retrieves relevant text chunks using vector similarity. GraphRAG creates knowledge graphs from documents to understand entity relationships and context, enabling more sophisticated reasoning. RAGFlow and R2R support GraphRAG approaches for complex query resolution requiring relational understanding.\n\n**How do I deploy RAG applications to production?**\n\nMost frameworks offer Docker deployment for easy scaling. Managed solutions like SciPhi Cloud (R2R) and Dify Cloud eliminate infrastructure concerns. For self-hosting, frameworks like Haystack and LangChain provide production-ready features including monitoring, authentication, and API layers.\n"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"9a:[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"container\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"border-b border-border-faint relative left-[calc(50%-50vw)] w-screen lg:hidden\"}],[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"h-42 p-30 border-b border-border-faint relative\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"svg\",null,{\"fill\":\"none\",\"height\":\"21\",\"viewBox\":\"0 0 22 21\",\"width\":\"22\",\"xmlns\":\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\",\"className\":\"pointer-events-none contain-[layout,paint] absolute -right-[10.5px] -top-11\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"path\",null,{\"d\":\"M10.5 4C10.5 7.31371 7.81371 10 4.5 10H0.5V11H4.5C7.81371 11 10.5 13.6863 10.5 17V21H11.5V17C11.5 13.6863 14.1863 11 17.5 11H21.5V10H17.5C14.1863 10 11.5 7.31371 11.5 4V0H10.5V4Z\",\"fill\":\"var(--border-faint)\"}]}],[\"$\",\"svg\",null,{\"fill\":\"none\",\"height\":\"21\",\"viewBox\":\"0 0 22 21\",\"width\":\"22\",\"xmlns\":\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\",\"className\":\"pointer-events-none contain-[layout,paint] absolute -left-[10.5px] -top-11\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"path\",null,{\"d\":\"M10.5 4C10.5 7.31371 7.81371 10 4.5 10H0.5V11H4.5C7.81371 11 10.5 13.6863 10.5 17V21H11.5V17C11.5 13.6863 14.1863 11 17.5 11H21.5V10H17.5C14.1863 10 11.5 7.31371 11.5 4V0H10.5V4Z\",\"fill\":\"var(--border-faint)\"}]}],[\"$\",\"svg\",null,{\"fill\":\"none\",\"height\":\"21\",\"viewBox\":\"0 0 11 21\",\"width\":\"11\",\"xmlns\":\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\",\"className\":\"pointer-events-none contain-[layout,paint] absolute left-0 -bottom-11\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"path\",null,{\"d\":\"M1 4C1 7.31371 3.68629 10 7 10H11V11H7C3.68629 11 1 13.6863 1 17V21H0V0H1V4Z\",\"fill\":\"var(--border-faint)\"}]}],[\"$\",\"svg\",null,{\"fill\":\"none\",\"height\":\"21\",\"viewBox\":\"0 0 11 21\",\"width\":\"11\",\"xmlns\":\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\",\"className\":\"pointer-events-none contain-[layout,paint] absolute right-0 -bottom-11\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"path\",null,{\"d\":\"M11 21H10V17C10 13.6863 7.31371 11 4 11H0V10H4C7.31371 10 10 7.31371 10 4V0H11V21Z\",\"fill\":\"var(--border-faint)\"}]}]]}],[\"$\",\"$L9c\",null,{\"author\":\"$3e:4:props:children:0:props:author\",\"posts\":[{\"title\":\"Add AI-Powered Data Retrieval to Your Product with Firecrawl\",\"description\":\"Learn how to add web data retrieval to your product using Firecrawl's Python SDK. Covers four patterns with code examples - search, scrape, crawl, and agent-driven gathering.\",\"slug\":\"ai-powered-data-retrieval\",\"date\":\"Mar 03, 2026\",\"author\":\"bex-tuychiev\",\"image\":\"/images/blog/ai-powered-data-retrieval/ai-powered-data-retrieval.webp\",\"categories\":[\"AI Engineering\",\"Web Extraction Mastery\"],\"keywords\":[\"ai search\",\"searching for ai\",\"searching for agents\",\"web search api\"],\"faqs\":[{\"question\":\"How is Firecrawl different from a SERP API like SerpAPI?\",\"answer\":\"SERP APIs return search metadata: titles, URLs, snippets. Firecrawl returns the actual page content as markdown or structured JSON. You can also search and scrape in a single call, extract data with schemas, or let the agent endpoint research a topic without specifying URLs.\"},{\"question\":\"Does Firecrawl have a Node SDK?\",\"answer\":\"Yes. The package is @mendable/firecrawl-js on npm. It covers the same endpoints as the Python SDK with equivalent methods and response types, so the patterns in this tutorial translate directly to TypeScript/JavaScript.\"},{\"question\":\"Can I use Firecrawl for RAG?\",\"answer\":\"Yes. The /search and /crawl endpoints return markdown that's ready to chunk and index. The /scrape endpoint's JSON extraction mode can pull structured metadata alongside the content, which is useful for filtering and retrieval ranking.\"},{\"question\":\"How do I handle pages that require authentication?\",\"answer\":\"The /scrape endpoint accepts custom headers (including cookies and auth tokens) for pages behind login walls. This doesn't apply to /search or /agent, which work through Firecrawl's own crawling infrastructure. For pages that need interactive login (clicking buttons, filling forms), use the browser sandbox instead.\"},{\"question\":\"Does Firecrawl render JavaScript?\",\"answer\":\"Yes. All endpoints render JavaScript by default. Single-page apps, client-side rendered content, and dynamically loaded elements work without extra configuration.\"},{\"question\":\"What's the difference between /agent and /extract?\",\"answer\":\"/extract is in maintenance mode and will be deprecated. /agent is its replacement and does everything /extract did (structured extraction from URLs) plus open-ended research without URLs. For new code, use /agent or /scrape with JSON extraction mode.\"},{\"question\":\"Can I self-host Firecrawl?\",\"answer\":\"Yes. Firecrawl is open source and available on GitHub. You can run it locally or deploy it to your own infrastructure. The self-hosted version uses the same API, so your code works with both hosted and self-hosted instances.\"},{\"question\":\"How do I use Firecrawl with MCP?\",\"answer\":\"Run npx firecrawl-mcp with your API key set as an environment variable. This starts a local MCP server that exposes 12 Firecrawl tools (scrape, crawl, search, agent, map, browser, and job management) to any MCP-compatible agent platform. Firecrawl also offers a hosted MCP endpoint if you don't want to run the server locally.\"}],\"body\":\"$9d\"},{\"title\":\"How to Build a Real-Time Voice Assistant with Gemini Live API and Firecrawl\",\"description\":\"Build a voice assistant that searches the web and manages Gmail using Gemini Live's native audio mode, LiveKit Agents for WebRTC transport, and Firecrawl for live web search — all in a single Python file.\",\"slug\":\"real-time-voice-assistant-gemini-firecrawl\",\"date\":\"Feb 23, 2026\",\"author\":\"bex-tuychiev\",\"image\":\"/images/blog/real-time-voice-assistant-gemini-firecrawl/real-time-voice-assistant-gemini-firecrawl.webp\",\"categories\":[\"Use Cases \u0026 Examples\",\"AI Engineering\",\"Workflows \u0026 Automation\"],\"keywords\":[\"gemini-live-api\",\"voice-assistant\",\"firecrawl\",\"livekit-agents\",\"native-audio\",\"real-time-voice\",\"gmail-voice-agent\",\"webrtc-agent\",\"gemini-2.5-flash\"],\"faqs\":[{\"question\":\"What is native audio mode in Gemini Live and how does it differ from cascaded mode?\",\"answer\":\"Native audio mode sends raw audio directly into and out of the model. Cascaded mode uses a separate speech-to-text model to transcribe your voice, sends the text to the LLM, then runs the response through a text-to-speech model. Native mode has lower latency and produces more natural-sounding responses because the model processes audio as a first-class input rather than a text conversion.\"},{\"question\":\"Does the audio stream disconnect when the agent calls a tool like web search?\",\"answer\":\"No. Gemini Live keeps the WebSocket connection open during tool execution. The model fires the function call, waits for the result, and speaks the answer over the same stream. You might hear a short pause while the search runs, but the session never drops.\"},{\"question\":\"How much does the Firecrawl search API cost for a voice assistant?\",\"answer\":\"Firecrawl's free tier includes 500 credits, and each search call costs 1 credit. For a personal voice assistant, that covers a few hundred searches per month. Paid plans start at $19/month with 3,000 credits. Check the pricing page for current rates.\"},{\"question\":\"Can I use a different LLM instead of Gemini for the voice agent?\",\"answer\":\"LiveKit Agents supports multiple model providers, including OpenAI's realtime API. The agent architecture stays the same; you swap the RealtimeModel configuration for a different plugin. Gemini's native audio mode and Firecrawl tool calling work the same way regardless of the transport layer.\"},{\"question\":\"Is the Gmail App Password approach secure enough for production?\",\"answer\":\"App Passwords bypass 2FA for SMTP/IMAP access, which is fine for a personal assistant running on your own machine or server. For a multi-user production app, you'd want OAuth 2.0 through the Gmail API instead. The App Password approach in this tutorial is scoped to a single account and doesn't expose your main Google password.\"}],\"body\":\"$9e\"},{\"title\":\"OpenClaw Guide: Build a Personal Agent with Internet Access Using Firecrawl\",\"description\":\"Everything you need to know about OpenClaw (formerly ClawdBot, MoltBot): what it is, how to set it up safely, and how to add live web search and scraping through Firecrawl.\",\"slug\":\"openclaw-firecrawl-guide\",\"date\":\"Feb 17, 2026\",\"updated\":\"Feb 28, 2026\",\"author\":\"bex-tuychiev\",\"image\":\"/images/blog/openclaw-firecrawl-guide/openclaw-firecrawl-guide.webp\",\"categories\":[\"AI Engineering\"],\"keywords\":[\"openclaw\",\"openclaw-tutorial\",\"self-hosted-ai-agent\",\"openclaw-firecrawl\",\"firecrawl\",\"ai-agent-web-scraping\",\"openclaw-ec2\",\"clawhub-skills\"],\"faqs\":[{\"question\":\"How much does it cost to run OpenClaw on EC2?\",\"answer\":\"A t4g.small instance (2 vCPU, 2GB RAM) costs about $15/month on AWS. Add 30GB of storage for another $2-3. With a Claude Pro or Max subscription (which you can use instead of per-token API billing), total monthly cost is under $30. A Mac Mini runs $500-600 upfront but has no recurring compute cost.\"},{\"question\":\"Can I use OpenClaw with local models instead of Claude?\",\"answer\":\"Yes. OpenClaw supports local models through Ollama. Install Ollama on your machine (or EC2 instance), pull a model like Llama or DeepSeek, and point OpenClaw at it in the config. The tradeoff is that smaller local models are not as sharp, and Steinberger himself warns they can be \\\"very gullible\\\" from a security standpoint.\"},{\"question\":\"Is my data sent to Firecrawl's servers?\",\"answer\":\"When you use the Firecrawl skill or API key integration, the URLs your agent scrapes are sent to Firecrawl's API for processing. The page content is returned to your agent and stored locally. Firecrawl doesn't store the scraped content long-term, but check their privacy policy for current terms. If this is a concern, Firecrawl also offers a self-hosted option you can run on your own infrastructure.\"},{\"question\":\"How do I clean up stale conversation data?\",\"answer\":\"Use the /compact command in chat to summarize and compress the current conversation context. For a full reset, /new starts a fresh thread. To clean up old heartbeat logs and cron job data, check ~/.openclaw/workspace/ for accumulated files and prune anything outdated. Running openclaw doctor --deep periodically flags storage and performance issues before they slow your agent down.\"},{\"question\":\"What messaging apps does OpenClaw support?\",\"answer\":\"Telegram, WhatsApp, Slack, Discord, Signal, and iMessage all connect through the gateway. Telegram is the most popular choice because it supports bot slash commands, tokens are free through @BotFather, and sessions don't expire. WhatsApp works but needs re-authentication every few days. Signal support is still experimental.\"},{\"question\":\"How secure is running an agent with full system access?\",\"answer\":\"It's a legitimate concern. OpenClaw has had reported vulnerabilities including exposed control panels and credentials stored in plaintext. Run it on a dedicated machine rather than your daily driver, bind the gateway to localhost, and audit your setup with openclaw doctor --deep regularly. Avoid installing unverified skills from ClawHub without reviewing the source.\"},{\"question\":\"What does Firecrawl add beyond OpenClaw's built-in web access?\",\"answer\":\"OpenClaw's built-in web_fetch uses a basic HTTP client that gets blocked by JavaScript-heavy sites, anti-bot protection, and paywalls. Firecrawl routes requests through a real browser, returning actual page content instead of empty HTML or 403 errors. The full skill also adds web search, site-wide crawling, and structured data extraction that the built-in tools can't do. For workflows that span multiple pages, the agent endpoint can gather data across an entire site in a single call.\"}],\"body\":\"$9f\"},{\"title\":\"How to Build a Web Scraping Agent With LangGraph and Firecrawl\",\"description\":\"Learn how to build a web scraping agent in Python using LangGraph and Firecrawl. The agent uses a ReAct loop to combine scraping, screenshots, structured extraction, web search, and documentation crawling based on plain English commands.\",\"slug\":\"web-scraping-agent-langgraph-firecrawl\",\"date\":\"Feb 4, 2026\",\"author\":\"bex-tuychiev\",\"image\":\"/images/blog/web-scraping-agent-langgraph-firecrawl/web-scraping-agent-langgraph-firecrawl.webp\",\"categories\":[\"AI Engineering\",\"Web Extraction Mastery\"],\"keywords\":[\"LangGraph\",\"Firecrawl\",\"web scraping agent\",\"ReAct\",\"Python\",\"scraping\",\"web search\",\"structured extraction\",\"crawl\",\"agent loop\",\"LangChain\",\"LLM tools\"],\"faqs\":[{\"question\":\"How does a web scraping agent differ from a traditional scraping script?\",\"answer\":\"A traditional script follows hardcoded instructions: specific URLs, CSS selectors, and data transformations. When a website changes its structure, the script breaks. An agent uses an LLM to reason about pages at runtime, deciding which tools to call and how to extract data based on natural language instructions rather than brittle selectors.\"},{\"question\":\"Can I use a different LLM instead of GPT-5?\",\"answer\":\"Yes. The agent uses LangChain's ChatOpenAI class, but you can swap it for any LangChain-compatible model. Anthropic's Claude, Google's Gemini, or local models via Ollama all work with minimal changes. Just replace the model initialization and ensure the model supports tool calling.\"},{\"question\":\"How much does it cost to run the web scraping agent?\",\"answer\":\"Costs depend on your LLM provider and usage. Following this tutorial with GPT-5 costs under $1. Firecrawl's free tier includes 500 credits. For production use, expect LLM costs of a few cents per complex query and Firecrawl costs based on pages scraped.\"},{\"question\":\"Is the run_python tool safe to use?\",\"answer\":\"For local development, yes. The tool uses Python's exec() with full builtins access, which means it can read and write files on your system. Never expose this tool over a network or let untrusted users interact with the agent. For production deployments, consider sandboxing code execution or removing the tool entirely.\"},{\"question\":\"Why use LangGraph instead of plain LangChain?\",\"answer\":\"LangGraph provides better control over agent loops through its functional API. The @task and @entrypoint decorators make it easy to define discrete operations and orchestrate them in a ReAct pattern. Plain LangChain agents work but offer less visibility into the reasoning process and fewer options for customization.\"},{\"question\":\"How do I add new tools to the agent?\",\"answer\":\"Create a function with the @tool decorator from langchain_core.tools. Write a clear docstring explaining what the tool does and when to use it. Add the function to the tools list and update the system prompt to mention the new capability. The LLM learns tool behavior from docstrings, so be specific.\"},{\"question\":\"What websites can the agent scrape?\",\"answer\":\"Firecrawl handles JavaScript rendering and common bot detection, so the agent works on most public websites. Some sites with aggressive anti-bot measures may block requests. The agent cannot access pages behind logins unless you implement authentication in your tools.\"},{\"question\":\"How do I handle rate limits and errors?\",\"answer\":\"The call_tool task already catches exceptions and returns error messages to the LLM, which can then try a different approach instead of crashing. For rate limits, add delays between tool calls or implement exponential backoff in your Firecrawl wrapper functions. You can also reduce the limit parameter on search and crawl operations.\"}],\"body\":\"$a0\"},{\"title\":\"Data Enrichment: A Complete Guide to Enhancing Your Data Quality\",\"description\":\"A comprehensive guide to enriching your data quality using modern tools, best practices, and real-world examples.\",\"slug\":\"complete-guide-to-data-enrichment\",\"date\":\"Jan 13, 2026\",\"updated\":\"Feb 09, 2026\",\"author\":\"bex-tuychiev\",\"image\":\"/images/blog/data_enrichment_guide/data-enrichment.webp\",\"categories\":[\"Use Cases \u0026 Examples\"],\"keywords\":[\"data enrichment\",\"data enrichment tools\",\"data enrichment services\",\"what is data enrichment\",\"b2b data enrichment\",\"data enrichment exposure\",\"data enrichment companies\",\"crm data enrichment\",\"data enrichment examples\",\"data enrichment definition\"],\"faqs\":[{\"question\":\"What is data enrichment and why does it matter?\",\"answer\":\"Data enrichment enhances existing datasets by adding valuable information from multiple sources, transforming basic data into actionable insights. It's crucial for improving customer profiles, marketing campaigns, and business decisions while maintaining competitive advantage.\"},{\"question\":\"How does Firecrawl differ from traditional web scraping tools?\",\"answer\":\"Firecrawl uses AI-powered extraction with natural language descriptions instead of brittle HTML selectors. This means scrapers remain stable when websites update, reducing maintenance overhead and enabling semantic understanding of content across different layouts.\"},{\"question\":\"What are the main types of data enrichment services?\",\"answer\":\"The six main categories are: demographic (age, income, education), behavioral (purchase patterns, interactions), company (firmographics, tech stack), psychographic (values, interests), geographic (location intelligence), and contact data (emails, social profiles).\"},{\"question\":\"How much does data enrichment typically cost?\",\"answer\":\"Costs vary significantly by solution: pay-per-lookup ranges from $0.05-0.25 per record, monthly subscriptions run $500-5,000, and enterprise contracts start at $50,000+ annually. Firecrawl offers flexible pricing from $0-100/month for web scraping needs.\"},{\"question\":\"What's the difference between B2B and B2C data enrichment?\",\"answer\":\"B2B enrichment focuses on company information, decision-makers, and firmographics with 85-95% match rates. B2C targets individual customer demographics and behavior with 70-85% match rates. Both require different tools and strategies.\"},{\"question\":\"How often should enriched data be refreshed?\",\"answer\":\"Best practice recommends refreshing enriched data every 3-6 months to maintain accuracy. However, rapidly changing information like contact details, pricing, or availability status may require real-time or monthly updates for optimal data quality.\"},{\"question\":\"Can Firecrawl handle large-scale data enrichment projects?\",\"answer\":\"Yes. Firecrawl supports both synchronous batch scraping and asynchronous API calls for large-scale projects. The /agent endpoint with parallel processing can batch hundreds or thousands of queries simultaneously, making it ideal for enriching datasets at scale.\"},{\"question\":\"What are common challenges in implementing data enrichment?\",\"answer\":\"Key challenges include maintaining data quality across sources (target \u003e95% accuracy), handling API rate limits, managing costs at scale, ensuring GDPR/CCPA compliance, dealing with website structure changes, and integrating enriched data with existing systems.\"}],\"body\":\"$a1\"},{\"title\":\"Top 9 Browser Automation Tools for Web Testing and Scraping in 2026\",\"description\":\"Comprehensive comparison of the best browser automation frameworks including Selenium, Playwright, Puppeteer, and Cypress for web testing, data extraction, and workflow automation with implementation guides.\",\"slug\":\"browser-automation-tools-comparison\",\"date\":\"Jan 12, 2026\",\"updated\":\"Feb 28, 2026\",\"author\":\"bex-tuychiev\",\"image\":\"/images/blog/browser-automation-tools/browser-automation-tools.webp\",\"categories\":[\"Web Extraction Mastery\"],\"keywords\":[\"browser automation tools\",\"web testing frameworks\",\"selenium alternatives\",\"headless browsers\",\"web scraping tools\",\"test automation\",\"cypress vs playwright\",\"puppeteer tutorial\",\"no-code automation\",\"web automation 2026\",\"web automation 2025\"],\"faqs\":[{\"question\":\"What are the main categories of browser automation tools?\",\"answer\":\"Browser automation tools can be grouped into headless browser solutions for server/CI tasks; full-browser automation frameworks for visible user interaction; and no-code/low-code platforms for non-developers.\"},{\"question\":\"Why is browser automation such a big deal in 2026?\",\"answer\":\"In 2026, AI agents need to do more than just search and scrape -they need to navigate multi-step flows, fill forms, handle auth, and interact with dynamic pages. Browser automation is the foundation that makes agentic workflows possible at scale. Tools like Claude Code's new preview feature and Firecrawl's Browser Sandbox reflect how rapidly the space is evolving.\"},{\"question\":\"What makes Firecrawl different from other browser automation tools?\",\"answer\":\"Firecrawl is open-source and built specifically for AI workflows. Its /agent endpoint uses AI to automate complex web data gathering -describe what you need in natural language, and it handles the rest. The new Browser Sandbox adds a fully managed, isolated browser environment so agents can navigate, click, and interact with the web without any local setup.\"},{\"question\":\"What is Firecrawl Browser Sandbox?\",\"answer\":\"Browser Sandbox is Firecrawl's secure, fully managed browser environment for AI agents. Each session runs in an isolated container with Playwright and Agent Browser pre-installed. There's zero configuration required -call it from the CLI or API and your agent has a live browser in seconds. Pricing is 2 credits per browser minute, with free users getting 5 hours of free usage.\"},{\"question\":\"Can Firecrawl be integrated into existing data pipelines or applications?\",\"answer\":\"Yes. Firecrawl offers SDKs for Python, Node.js, Go, and Rust. It integrates with LangChain, LlamaIndex, and other AI frameworks. Use the API to plug into data pipelines, CRMs, or AI agents for automated data collection.\"},{\"question\":\"When should a team choose a headless browser solution like Puppeteer?\",\"answer\":\"Headless tools suit server-side environments/CI pipelines where UI isn't needed, and you want resource-efficient automation for tasks such as scraping, screenshotting or large-scale workflows.\"},{\"question\":\"Why might a team pick a framework like Playwright over more established tools?\",\"answer\":\"Playwright offers true cross-browser automation (Chromium, Firefox, WebKit), multi-language SDKs, and features like auto-waits and tracing to combat flakiness -ideal for modern, complex automation needs.\"},{\"question\":\"What are the advantages of no-code/low-code automation tools like Browserflow or Bardeen AI?\",\"answer\":\"These tools empower non-technical users with drag-and-drop visual editors, pre-built templates, and integrations, enabling workflow automation and data extraction without deep programming knowledge.\"},{\"question\":\"How should you decide which browser automation tool is right for your project?\",\"answer\":\"Align tool choice with your primary goal (testing vs. scraping vs. workflow automation), technical expertise, supported browsers/languages, and budget -then pick the tool whose strengths match your needs.\"},{\"question\":\"What future trends in browser automation are highlighted in the blog?\",\"answer\":\"The blog emphasises AI-driven automation (self-healing scripts, autonomous workflows), increased focus on mobile and multi-device coverage, managed browser sandboxes for AI agents, Claude Code's preview capabilities, and the ethical/data-privacy implications of widespread automation.\"}],\"body\":\"$a2\"},{\"title\":\"How to Create a Claude Code Skill: A Web Scraping Example with Firecrawl\",\"description\":\"Learn how to build a Claude Code skill that adds web scraping capabilities using Firecrawl. This tutorial covers skill structure, YAML frontmatter, triggering behavior, and building a multi-feature skill for markdown extraction, screenshots, structured data, web search, and documentation crawling.\",\"slug\":\"claude-code-skill\",\"date\":\"Jan 9, 2026\",\"updated\":\"Mar 01, 2026\",\"author\":\"bex-tuychiev\",\"image\":\"/images/blog/claude-code-skill/hero.webp\",\"categories\":[\"AI Engineering\"],\"keywords\":[\"Claude Code skill tutorial\",\"Claude Code web scraping\",\"Claude Code automation\",\"SKILL.md\"],\"faqs\":[{\"question\":\"My Claude Code skill isn't triggering. What's wrong?\",\"answer\":\"Check three things. First, restart Claude Code. Skills load at startup, so changes won't appear until you exit and run `claude` again. Second, run `/skills` to confirm your skill appears in the list. If it's missing, check that your `SKILL.md` file has valid YAML frontmatter with both `name` and `description` fields. Third, review your description. Vague descriptions like \\\"helps with web stuff\\\" rarely trigger. Add specific phrases users would actually say.\"},{\"question\":\"Do I need to restart Claude Code after every change?\",\"answer\":\"Yes. Claude Code reads the skills directory once at startup. Any edits to `SKILL.md` or your scripts require a restart. Exit your session and run `claude` again to load the updated skill.\"},{\"question\":\"How do I keep my API key secure?\",\"answer\":\"Store it in `~/.env` or export it in your shell profile. Never hardcode keys in your `SKILL.md` or Python scripts. The `python-dotenv` package loads environment variables automatically, and the key stays outside version control.\"},{\"question\":\"Do I need a paid Firecrawl plan to build a Claude Code skill?\",\"answer\":\"No, the free tier includes 500 credits, which should cover creating your first Claude Code skill. Scraping one page costs one credit. Screenshots, markdown extraction, and structured extraction all cost one credit per URL. Search costs one credit per query. Crawling costs one credit per page crawled. For this tutorial, you'll use around 20-50 credits depending on how much you test.\"},{\"question\":\"Can I share this skill with my team?\",\"answer\":\"Yes. Move the skill folder from `~/.claude/skills/` to `.claude/skills/` in your project repository and commit it. Anyone who clones the repo gets the skill automatically. Team members will need their own Firecrawl API keys stored in their local environment.\"},{\"question\":\"What's the difference between skills and MCP servers?\",\"answer\":\"MCP servers give Claude raw access to an API's tools. Skills teach Claude how to use those tools with your preferences baked in. A Firecrawl MCP server exposes every endpoint with all parameters. This skill exposes five specific features with sensible defaults. Skills also load progressively, consuming fewer tokens than MCP servers that load everything upfront.\"},{\"question\":\"Can I use multiple Claude Code skills together?\",\"answer\":\"Yes, Claude can activate different skills for different parts of a conversation. If you have a Firecrawl skill and a PDF skill, Claude will use Firecrawl for web requests and the PDF skill for document processing. Skills don't conflict as long as their descriptions target different use cases.\"},{\"question\":\"What's the difference between skills and slash commands?\",\"answer\":\"Slash commands are explicit. You type `/command` and Claude runs a predefined prompt. Skills, on the other hand, are implicit. Claude reads your request, matches it against skill descriptions, and activates the right one automatically. Use slash commands for actions you repeat verbatim, like `/commit` or `/review`. Use skills for capabilities Claude should discover on its own based on context. A Firecrawl slash command would require you to remember and type it. A Firecrawl skill activates when you say \\\"grab the markdown from this page\\\" without thinking about which tool to invoke.\"},{\"question\":\"Can I install pre-built skills instead of building from scratch?\",\"answer\":\"Yes. Anthropic maintains an official skills repository at github.com/anthropics/skills with production-ready skills for document creation (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX), Slack GIF creation, and more. Community collections like Superpowers add structured workflow skills for brainstorming, planning, systematic debugging, and verification. Install any of them with `/plugin add marketplace https://github.com/anthropics/skills`. Building from scratch is still useful if you need specific behavior, custom defaults, or integration with a private API.\"},{\"question\":\"Do Claude Code skills work with other AI coding tools besides Claude Code?\",\"answer\":\"Yes. Skills rely on filesystem access and the ability to run commands, not on Claude Code specifically. Any coding agent with those capabilities, including Codex CLI and Gemini CLI, can read the same SKILL.md files without modification. You write a skill once and it works wherever the model can read files and execute scripts.\"},{\"question\":\"What's the difference between a skill and a plugin?\",\"answer\":\"Skills are markdown files you create or copy into `~/.claude/skills/`. They trigger automatically based on context, load progressively, and encode specific workflows with your preferences baked in. No installation step required. Plugins are packaged integrations installed through Claude Code's plugin system. They bundle MCP servers, tools, and sometimes skills into a single install and are designed for distribution. Use a skill when you want control over a specific workflow. Use a plugin when you want a maintained, pre-packaged integration without the setup overhead.\"}],\"body\":\"$a3\"},{\"title\":\"Best Open-Source Web Crawlers in 2026\",\"description\":\"Compare the top 10 open-source web crawlers for 2026 including Firecrawl, Scrapy, Crawl4AI, and Playwright. Find the right crawler for your project with detailed pros, cons, and real-world benchmarks across Python, Go, Node.js, and Java.\",\"slug\":\"best-open-source-web-crawler\",\"date\":\"Jan 6, 2026\",\"author\":\"bex-tuychiev\",\"image\":\"/images/blog/os-web-crawlers/os-web-crawlers.webp\",\"categories\":[\"Web Extraction Mastery\"],\"keywords\":[\"open source web crawler\",\"best web crawler\",\"best site crawler\",\"web crawler open source\"],\"body\":\"$a4\"},{\"title\":\"Scraper vs Crawler: When to Use Each (With Examples)\",\"description\":\"Learn the difference between web scraping and web crawling, when to use each approach, and build two practical projects with Firecrawl: a job listing extractor and a blog content collector.\",\"slug\":\"scraper-vs-crawler\",\"date\":\"Jan 3, 2026\",\"author\":\"bex-tuychiev\",\"image\":\"/images/blog/scraper-vs-crawler/scraper-vs-crawler.webp\",\"categories\":[\"Web Extraction Mastery\"],\"keywords\":[\"scraper vs crawler\",\"web scraping vs web crawling\",\"web scraper\",\"web crawler\",\"firecrawl\"],\"body\":\"$a5\"},{\"title\":\"15 Best Open-Source RAG Frameworks in 2026\",\"description\":\"Discover the top open-source retrieval-augmented generation frameworks that enhance LLM capabilities with external knowledge retrieval for more accurate and contextual AI responses.\",\"slug\":\"best-open-source-rag-frameworks\",\"date\":\"Jan 2, 2026\",\"author\":\"bex-tuychiev\",\"image\":\"/images/blog/os-rag-frameworks/os-rag-libs.webp\",\"categories\":[\"AI Engineering\"],\"keywords\":[\"best rag frameworks\",\"open-source rag frameworks\",\"rag frameworks\",\"retrieval augmented generation\",\"vector databases\",\"knowledge retrieval\"],\"body\":\"$a6\"}]}]]}]\n"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"96:[[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"!text-title-h5 [\u0026:not(:first-child)]:mt-72 mb-24 relative\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"span\",null,{\"className\":\"absolute -top-[120px]\",\"id\":\"introduction\"}],[\"$\",\"h2\",null,{\"className\":\"!text-title-h5\",\"children\":\"Introduction\"}]]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"Web scraping is an essential skill for programmers in this data-driven world. Whether you're tracking prices, monitoring competitors, or gathering research data, automated web scraping can save you countless hours of manual work. In this comprehensive guide, you'll learn how to schedule and automate your Python web scrapers using completely free tools and services. If you're building a scraper that targets paginated lists or product grids, check out our guide on \",[\"$\",\"$L26\",null,{\"href\":\"/blog/list-crawling-python-beautifulsoup-scrapy-firecrawl\",\"className\":\"!underline\",\"children\":\"list crawling with Python\"}],\" before automating it—getting the extraction right first makes scheduling far simpler.\"]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"blog-heading relative [\u0026:not(:first-child)]:mt-48 mb-16\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"span\",null,{\"className\":\"absolute -top-[120px]\",\"id\":\"why-automate-your-web-scraper?\"}],[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"children\":\"Why automate your web scraper?\"}]]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Manual or one-off web scraping can be time-consuming and error-prone. You need to repeatedly run scripts, update data frequently, and sometimes work during off-hours just to gather information.\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Scheduling your web scrapers automates this entire process. It collects data at optimal times without manual intervention, ensures consistency, and frees up your valuable time for actual data analysis rather than repetitive data gathering.\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"blog-heading relative [\u0026:not(:first-child)]:mt-48 mb-16\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"span\",null,{\"className\":\"absolute -top-[120px]\",\"id\":\"what-tools-can-you-use-to-automate-web-scrapers-in-python?\"}],[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"children\":\"What tools can you use to automate web scrapers in Python?\"}]]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"If you are building scrapers in Python, you have many completely free options to schedule them.\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"In terms of local scraping, Python's \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"schedule\"}],\" library is very intuitive and fastest to set up. There is also the built-in \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"asyncio\"}],\" for concurrent scraping. There are also system-level automation tools like cron jobs for macOS/Linux and the Task Scheduler for Windows.\"]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"There are also many cloud-based solutions like GitHub Actions (completely free), PythonAnywhere (free tier), Google Cloud Functions (free tier), and Heroku (free tier with limitations).\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"In this guide, we will start with basic local scheduling and progress to using GitHub Actions, all while following best practices and ethical scraping guidelines.\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Let's begin by setting up your development environment and writing your first scheduled web scraper.\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"blog-heading relative [\u0026:not(:first-child)]:mt-48 mb-16\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"span\",null,{\"className\":\"absolute -top-[120px]\",\"id\":\"common-challenges-in-automating-web-scrapers\"}],[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"children\":\"Common challenges in automating web scrapers\"}]]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Scheduling web scrapers is an easy process, as you will discover through this tutorial. The real challenge lies in ensuring that the scrapers don't break the day after they are put on schedule. Common issues include websites changing their HTML structure, implementing new anti-bot measures, or modifying their robots.txt policies. Additionally, network issues, rate limiting, and IP blocking can cause scheduled scrapers to fail.\"}],\"\\n\",\"$La7\",\"\\n\",\"$La8\",\"\\n\",\"$La9\",\"\\n\",\"$Laa\",\"\\n\",\"$Lab\",\"\\n\",\"$Lac\",\"\\n\",\"$Lad\",\"\\n\",\"$Lae\",\"\\n\",\"$Laf\",\"\\n\",\"$Lb0\",\"\\n\",\"$Lb1\",\"\\n\",\"$Lb2\",\"\\n\",\"$Lb3\",\"\\n\",\"$Lb4\",\"\\n\",\"$Lb5\",\"\\n\",\"$Lb6\",\"\\n\",\"$Lb7\",\"\\n\",\"$Lb8\",\"\\n\",\"$Lb9\",\"\\n\",\"$Lba\",\"\\n\",\"$Lbb\",\"\\n\",\"$Lbc\",\"\\n\",\"$Lbd\",\"\\n\",\"$Lbe\",\"\\n\",\"$Lbf\",\"\\n\",\"$Lc0\",\"\\n\",\"$Lc1\",\"\\n\",\"$Lc2\",\"\\n\",\"$Lc3\",\"\\n\",\"$Lc4\",\"\\n\",\"$Lc5\",\"\\n\",\"$Lc6\",\"\\n\",\"$Lc7\",\"\\n\",\"$Lc8\",\"\\n\",\"$Lc9\",\"\\n\",\"$Lca\",\"\\n\",\"$Lcb\",\"\\n\",\"$Lcc\",\"\\n\",\"$Lcd\",\"\\n\",\"$Lce\",\"\\n\",\"$Lcf\",\"\\n\",\"$Ld0\",\"\\n\",\"$Ld1\",\"\\n\",\"$Ld2\",\"\\n\",\"$Ld3\",\"\\n\",\"$Ld4\",\"\\n\",\"$Ld5\",\"\\n\",\"$Ld6\",\"\\n\",\"$Ld7\",\"\\n\",\"$Ld8\",\"\\n\",\"$Ld9\",\"\\n\",\"$Lda\",\"\\n\",\"$Ldb\",\"\\n\",\"$Ldc\",\"\\n\",\"$Ldd\",\"\\n\",\"$Lde\",\"\\n\",\"$Ldf\",\"\\n\",\"$Le0\",\"\\n\",\"$Le1\",\"\\n\",\"$Le2\",\"\\n\",\"$Le3\",\"\\n\",\"$Le4\",\"\\n\",\"$Le5\",\"\\n\",\"$Le6\",\"\\n\",\"$Le7\",\"\\n\",\"$Le8\",\"\\n\",\"$Le9\",\"\\n\",\"$Lea\",\"\\n\",\"$Leb\",\"\\n\",\"$Lec\",\"\\n\",\"$Led\",\"\\n\",\"$Lee\",\"\\n\",\"$Lef\",\"\\n\",\"$Lf0\",\"\\n\",\"$Lf1\",\"\\n\",\"$Lf2\",\"\\n\",\"$Lf3\",\"\\n\",\"$Lf4\",\"\\n\",\"$Lf5\",\"\\n\",\"$Lf6\",\"\\n\",\"$Lf7\",\"\\n\",\"$Lf8\",\"\\n\",\"$Lf9\",\"\\n\",\"$Lfa\",\"\\n\",\"$Lfb\",\"\\n\",\"$Lfc\",\"\\n\",\"$Lfd\",\"\\n\",\"$Lfe\",\"\\n\",\"$Lff\",\"\\n\",\"$L100\",\"\\n\",\"$L101\",\"\\n\",\"$L102\",\"\\n\",\"$L103\",\"\\n\",\"$L104\",\"\\n\",\"$L105\",\"\\n\",\"$L106\",\"\\n\",\"$L107\",\"\\n\",\"$L108\",\"\\n\",\"$L109\",\"\\n\",\"$L10a\",\"\\n\",\"$L10b\",\"\\n\",\"$L10c\",\"\\n\",\"$L10d\",\"\\n\",\"$L10e\",\"\\n\",\"$L10f\",\"\\n\",\"$L110\",\"\\n\",\"$L111\",\"\\n\",\"$L112\",\"\\n\",\"$L113\",\"\\n\",\"$L114\",\"\\n\",\"$L115\",\"\\n\",\"$L116\",\"\\n\",\"$L117\",\"\\n\",\"$L118\",\"\\n\",\"$L119\",\"\\n\",\ ges/output.png\",\"image\"]\n:HL[\"/images/blog/scheduling-scrapers-images/actions.png\",\"image\"]\n:HL[\"/images/blog/scheduling-scrapers-images/finished_actions.png\",\"image\"]\na7:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"For these reasons, it is almost impossible to build long-lasting scrapers written in Python frameworks. But the web scraping landscape is changing as more AI-based tools are emerging, like \",[\"$\",\"$L26\",null,{\"href\":\"https://www.firecrawl.dev\",\"className\":\"!underline\",\"children\":\"Firecrawl\"}],\".\"]}]\na8:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Firecrawl provides an AI-powered web scraping API that can identify and extract data from HTML elements based on semantic descriptions in Python classes. While traditional scrapers rely on specific HTML selectors that can break when websites change, Firecrawl's AI approach helps maintain scraper reliability over time.\"}]\na9:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"For demonstration purposes, we'll implement examples using Firecrawl, though the scheduling techniques covered in this tutorial can be applied to any Python web scraper built with common libraries like BeautifulSoup, Scrapy, \",[\"$\",\"$L26\",null,{\"href\":\"/blog/browser-automation-tools-comparison\",\"className\":\"!underline\",\"children\":\"Selenium\"}],\", or \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"lxml\"}],\". If you want to follow along with a scraper of your own, make sure to have it in a script and ready to go.\"]}]\naa:[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"!text-title-h5 [\u0026:not(:first-child)]:mt-72 mb-24 relative\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"span\",null,{\"className\":\"absolute -top-[120px]\",\"id\":\"prerequisites\"}],[\"$\",\"h2\",null,{\"className\":\"!text-title-h5\",\"children\":\"Prerequisites\"}]]}]\nab:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"This article assumes that you are already comfortable with web scraping and its related concepts like HTML structure, CSS selectors, HTTP requests, and handling rate limits. validation and serialization capabilities. They allow you to define the structure and types of your data using Python type hints, while automatically handling validation, serialization, and documentation.\"}]\nc6:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"In the context of our Firecrawl scraper, the \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"NewsItem\"}],\" model defines the exact structure of data we want to extract from each Hacker News post. Each field in the model (\",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"title\"}],\", \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"source_url\"}],\", \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"author\"}],\", etc.) specifies what data should be scraped and includes a description of what that field represents.\"]}]\nc7:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"This model is crucial for Firecrawl because it uses the model's schema to understand exactly what data to extract from the webpage. When we pass this model to Firecrawl, it will automatically attempt to find and extract data matching these field definitions from the HTML structure of Hacker News.\"}]\nc8:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"For example, when Firecrawl sees we want a \\\"title\\\" field, it will look for elements on the page that are likely to contain post titles based on their HTML structure and content. The \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"Field\"}],\" descriptions help provide additional context about what each piece of data represents.\"]}]\nc9:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"Next, we create another model called \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"NewsData\"}],\" that contains a list of \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"NewsItem\"}],\" objects. This model will serve as a container for all the news items we scrape from Hacker News. The \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"news_items\"}],\" field is defined as a List of \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"NewsItem\"}],\" objects, which means it can store multiple news items in a single data structure.\"]}]\n"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"ca:[\"$\",\"figure\",null,{\"data-rehype-pretty-code-figure\":\"\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"relative\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"absolute Then, it generates a filename using the current timestamp in the format \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"YYYY_MM_DD_HH_MM\"}],\". The data is saved to this timestamped JSON file with proper indentation, and the filename is returned. Thanks to Firecrawl's AI-powered HTML parsing and layout adaptation capabilities, the scraper is quite resilient to website changes and has a low probability of breaking.\"]}]\nfa:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"Nevertheless, web scraping can be unpredictable, so it's recommended to review \",[\"$\",\"$L26\",null,{\"href\":\"https://schedule.readthedocs.io/en/stable/exception-handling.html\",\"className\":\"!underline\",\"children\":\"the exception handling\"}],\" section of the \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"schedule\"}],\" documentation to handle potential errors gracefully.\"]}]\nfb:[\"$\",\"hr\",null,{}]\nfc:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"The \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"schedule\"}],\" library provides a simple and intuitive way to run periodic tasks like web scrapers. While it lacks some advanced features of other scheduling methods, it's a great choice for basic scheduling needs and getting started with automated scraping. Just remember to implement proper error handling for production use.\"]}]\nfd:[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"blog-heading relative [\u0026:not(:first-child)]:mt-48 mb-16\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"span\",null,{\"className\":\"absolute -top-[120px]\",\"id\":\"using-python's-built-in-tools-to-automate-web-scrapers\"}],[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"children\":\"Using Python's built-in tools to automate web scrapers\"}]]}]\nfe:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"In this section, we will explore a few other local scheduling methods that have the advantage of being built into Python or the operating system, making them more reliable and robust than third-party libraries. These methods also provide better error handling, logging capabilities, and system-level control over the scheduling process.\"}]\nff:[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"blog-heading relative [\u0026:not(:first-child)]:mt-32 mb-8\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"span\",null,{\"className\":\"absolute -top-[120px]\",\"id\":\"how-to-automate-a-web-scraper-with-,[object-object],?\"}],[\"$\",\"h4\",null,{\"children\":[\"How to automate a web scraper with \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"asyncio\"}],\"?\"]}]]}]\n100:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"asyncio\"}],\" is a Python library for running code concurrently - executing multiple tasks at the same time by switching between them when one is waiting. It's built into Python and helps schedule tasks efficiently. Here's why it's great for web scrapers:\"]}]\n101:[\"$\",\"ol\",null,{\"children\":[\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"It can do other work while waiting for web requests to complete.\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"You can run multiple scrapers at the same time with precise timing control.\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"It uses less mb-8\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"span\",null,{\"className\":\"absolute -top-[120px]\",\"id\":\"how-to-automate-a-web-scraper-with-cron-jobs?\"}],[\"$\",\"h4\",null,{\"children\":\"How to automate a web scraper with cron jobs?\"}]]}]\n10c:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Cron is a time-based job scheduler in Unix-like operating systems (Linux, macOS). Think of it as a digital scheduler or calendar that can automatically run programs at specified times. A cron job is simply a task that you schedule to run at specific intervals.\"}]\n10d:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"For web scraping, cron jobs are incredibly useful because they let you automate your scraper to run at predetermined times. For example, you could set up a cron job to:\"}]\n10e:[\"$\",\"ul\",null,{\"children\":[\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Run your scraper every hour to collect real-time data\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Execute scraping tasks during off-peak hours (like 2 AM) to minimize server load\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Collect data at specific times when websites update their content\"}],\"\\n\"]}]\n10f:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"The scheduling format uses five time fields: minute, hour, day of month, month, and day of week. For instance:\"}]\n110:[\"$\",\"ul\",null,{\"children\":[\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":[[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"0 * * * *\"}],\" means \\\"run every hour at minute 0\\\"\"]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":[[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"0 0 * * *\"}],\" means \\\"run at midnight every day\\\"\"]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":[[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"*/15 * * * *\"}],\" means \\\"run every 15 minutes\\\"\"]}],\"\\n\"]}]\n111:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Cron jobs are especially reliable for web scraping because they're built into the operating system, use minimal resources, and continue running even after system reboots.\"}]\n112:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"So, let's run the \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"save_firecrawl_news_data()\"}],\" function on a schedule using cron. Specifies every minute\"]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":[\"First \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"*\"}],\": Represents any hour\"]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":[\"Second \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"*\"}],\": Represents any day of the month\"]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":[\"Fourth \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"*\"}],\": Represents any day of the -top-[120px]\",\"id\":\"automating-web-scrapers-with-github-actions\"}],[\"$\",\"h2\",null,{\"className\":\"!text-title-h5\",\"children\":\"Automating Web Scrapers With GitHub Actions\"}]]}]\n132:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"One disadvantage all local scheduling methods have is that they rely on your local machine being powered on and connected to the internet. If your computer is turned off, loses power, or loses internet connectivity, your scheduled scraping tasks won't run. This is where cloud-based solutions like GitHub Actions can provide more reliability and uptime for your web scraping workflows.\"}]\n133:[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"blog-heading relative [\u0026:not(:first-child)]:mt-48 mb-16\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"span\",null,{\"className\":\"absolute -top-[120px]\",\"id\":\"what-is-github-actions?\"}],[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"children\":\"What is GitHub Actions?\"}]]}]\n134:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"GitHub Actions is a continuous integration and deployment (CI/CD) platform provided by GitHub that allows you to automate various workflows directly from your \",[\"$\",\"$L26\",null,{\"href\":\"https://github.com\",\"className\":\"!underline\",\"children\":\"GitHub\"}],\" repository.\"]}]\n135:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"For web scraping, GitHub Actions provides a reliable way to schedule and run your scraping scripts in the cloud. Actions tab of your repository like below:\"}]\n14d:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"$\",\"img\",null,{\"src\":\"/images/blog/scheduling-scrapers-images/actions.png\",\"alt\":\"\\\"GitHub Actions workflow showing successful execution of scheduled web scraping with Firecrawl\\\"\"}]}]\n14e:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Click on the workflow name and press the \\\"Run workflow\\\" button. This launches the action manually and starts the schedule. If you check in after some time, you should see more automatic runs and the results persisted in your repository:\"}]\n14f:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"$\",\"img\",null,{\"src\":\"/images/blog/scheduling-scrapers-images/finished_actions.png\",\"alt\":\"\\\"GitHub Actions workflow showing multiple successful executions of scheduled web scraping with Firecrawl over a 24 hour period\\\"\"}]}]\n150:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Caution: I left the workflow running overnight (at five minute intervals) and was nastily surprised by 96 workflow runs the next day. Thankfully, GitHub actions are free (up to 2000 min/month) unlike AWS instances.\"}]\n151:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Now, unless you disable the workflow manually by clicking the three dots in the upper-right corner, the scraper continues running on the schedule you specified.\"}]\n152:[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"!text-title-h5 [\u0026:not(:first-child)]:mt-72 mb-24 relative\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"span\",null,{\"className\":\"absolute -top-[120px]\",\"id\":\"best-practices-and-optimization\"}],[\"$\",\"h2\",null,{\"className\":\"!text-title-h5\",\"children\":\"Best Practices and Optimization\"}]]}]\n153:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"When scheduling web scrapers, following best practices ensures reliability, efficiency, and ethical behavior. Here are the key areas to consider:\"}]\n154:[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"blog-heading relative [\u0026:not(:first-child)]:mt-48 mb-16\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"span\",null,{\"className\":\"absolute -top-[120px]\",\"id\":\"1.-rate-limiting-and-delays\"}],[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"children\":\"1. Rate limiting and delays\"}]]}]\n155:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Scraping engines like Firecrawl usually come with built-in rate limiting. However, if you are using custom scrapers written with Python libraries, you must always respect website servers by implementing proper rate limiting and delay strategies. For example, the below example shows adding random delays between your requests in-between requests to respect server load and avoid getting your IP blocked:\"}]\n"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"156:[\"$\",\"figure\",null,{\"data-rehype-pretty-code-figure\":\"\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"relative\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"absolute
